features.name: Tool Proficiency
features.description: You gain proficiency with alchemist's supplies. If you already have this proficiency, you gain proficiency with one other type of artisan's tools of your choice.

features.name: Alchemist Spells
features.description: You always have certain spells prepared after you reach particular levels in this class, as shown in the Alchemist Spells table. These count as artificer spells for you, but don't count against the number you prepare.

features.name: Experimental Elixir
features.description: Whenever you finish a long rest, you can magically produce an experimental elixir in an empty flask. Roll on the Experimental Elixir table for its effect. You can create additional elixirs by expending a spell slot of 1st level or higher. Creating an elixir requires alchemist supplies on your person.

features.name: Alchemical Savant
features.description: Whenever you cast a spell using your alchemist's supplies as the spellcasting focus, you gain a bonus to one roll of the spell that restores hit points or deals acid, fire, necrotic, or poison damage. The bonus equals your Intelligence modifier (minimum of +1).

features.name: Restorative Reagents
features.description: Whenever a creature drinks an experimental elixir you created, it gains temporary hit points equal to 2d6 + your Intelligence modifier. You can cast lesser restoration without expending a spell slot a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier, regaining all uses after a long rest.

features.name: Chemical Mastery
features.description: You gain resistance to acid damage and poison damage, and are immune to the poisoned condition. You can cast greater restoration and heal without expending a spell slot, without preparing the spell, and without material components, provided you use alchemist's supplies as the spellcasting focus. Once you cast either spell with this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Tools of the Trade
features.description: You gain proficiency with heavy armor and with smith's tools. If you already have this tool proficiency, you gain proficiency with one other type of artisan's tools of your choice.

features.name: Armorer Spells
features.description: You always have certain spells prepared after you reach particular levels in this class, as shown in the Armorer Spells table. These count as artificer spells for you, but don't count against the number you prepare.

features.name: Arcane Armor
features.description: As an action, you can turn a suit of armor you are wearing into Arcane Armor. You gain benefits while wearing it: it lacks a Strength requirement for you, you can use it as a spellcasting focus, it attaches to you and can't be removed against your will, and you can don or doff it as an action.

features.name: Armor Model
features.description: You can customize your Arcane Armor, choosing either the Guardian or Infiltrator model. Each model gives you special benefits, including a special weapon (Thunder Gauntlets for Guardian, Lightning Launcher for Infiltrator). You can change the model after a short or long rest with smith's tools.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice, rather than once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Armor Modifications
features.description: Your Arcane Armor counts as separate items (chest, boots, helmet, weapon) for your Infuse Items feature. The maximum number of items you can infuse increases by 2, but those extra items must be part of your Arcane Armor.

features.name: Perfected Armor
features.description: Your Arcane Armor gains additional benefits based on its model. Guardian: As a reaction, you can pull a creature within 30 feet up to 30 feet towards you and make a melee weapon attack against it. Infiltrator: Creatures hit by your Lightning Launcher glimmer, have disadvantage on attacks against you, and the next attack roll against them has advantage and deals an extra 1d6 lightning damage.

features.name: Tool Proficiency
features.description: You gain proficiency with woodcarver's tools. If you already have this proficiency, you gain proficiency with one other type of artisan's tools of your choice.

features.name: Artillerist Spells
features.description: You always have certain spells prepared after you reach particular levels in this class, as shown in the Artillerist Spells table. These count as artificer spells for you, but don't count against the number you prepare.

features.name: Eldritch Cannon
features.description: You can use an action to magically create a Small or Tiny eldritch cannon in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you. The cannon is a magical object with AC 18 and HP equal to five times your artificer level. On each of your turns, you can take a bonus action to cause the cannon to activate, choosing from Flamethrower, Force Ballista, or Protector effects.

features.name: Arcane Firearm
features.description: You can turn a wand, staff, or rod into your arcane firearm. You can use it as a spellcasting focus. When you cast an artificer spell through it, roll a d8 and gain a bonus to one of the spell's damage rolls equal to the number rolled.

features.name: Explosive Cannon
features.description: Your eldritch cannon's damage rolls all increase by 1d8. As an action, you can command it to detonate, forcing creatures within 20 feet to make a Dexterity saving throw or take 3d8 force damage.

features.name: Fortified Position
features.description: You and your allies have half cover while within 10 feet of a cannon you create. You can now have two cannons at the same time, can create two with the same action, and can activate both with the same bonus action.

features.name: Tool Proficiency
features.description: You gain proficiency with smith's tools. If you already have this proficiency, you gain proficiency with one other type of artisan's tools of your choice.

features.name: Battle Smith Spells
features.description: You always have certain spells prepared after you reach particular levels in this class, as shown in the Battle Smith Spells table. These count as artificer spells for you, but don't count against the number you prepare.

features.name: Battle Ready
features.description: You gain proficiency with martial weapons. When you attack with a magic weapon, you can use your Intelligence modifier, instead of Strength or Dexterity, for the attack and damage rolls.

features.name: Steel Defender
features.description: You gain a steel defender companion. It is friendly to you and your companions and obeys your commands. In combat, it shares your initiative count and takes its turn immediately after yours. It can move and use its reaction on its own, but the only action it takes is Dodge unless you command it.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice, rather than once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Arcane Jolt
features.description: When you or your steel defender hit a target, you can channel magical energy to deal an extra 2d6 force damage to the target, or to restore 2d6 hit points to a creature or object you can see within 30 feet of the target. You can use this a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier, regaining all uses after a long rest.

features.name: Improved Defender
features.description: The extra damage and healing of your Arcane Jolt increase to 4d6. Your steel defender gains a +2 bonus to AC. Whenever your steel defender uses its Deflect Attack, the attacker takes force damage equal to 1d4 + your Intelligence modifier.

features.name: Frenzy
features.description: You can go into a frenzy when you rage. For the duration, you can make a single melee weapon attack as a bonus action on each of your turns. When your rage ends, you suffer one level of exhaustion.

features.name: Mindless Rage
features.description: You can't be charmed or frightened while raging. If you are charmed or frightened when you enter your rage, the effect is suspended for the duration.

features.name: Intimidating Presence
features.description: You can use your action to frighten a creature within 30 feet with your menacing presence. It must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be frightened of you until the end of your next turn. You can extend the duration on subsequent turns. Once a creature succeeds, you can't use this feature on it again for 24 hours.

features.name: Retaliation
features.description: When you take damage from a creature within 5 feet of you, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against that creature.

features.name: Spirit Seeker
features.description: You gain the ability to cast beast sense and speak with animals as rituals.

features.name: Totem Spirit
features.description: You choose a totem animal and gain its feature. You also make a physical totem object. Options: Bear (resistance to all damage except psychic while raging), Eagle (disadvantage on opportunity attacks against you and Dash as bonus action while raging), Wolf (friends have advantage on melee attacks against enemies within 5 feet of you while raging).

features.name: Aspect of the Beast
features.description: You gain a magical benefit based on a totem animal. Options: Bear (double carrying capacity and advantage on Strength checks to push/pull/lift), Eagle (see up to 1 mile, dim light no disadvantage on Perception), Wolf (track while traveling fast, move stealthily at normal pace).

features.name: Spirit Walker
features.description: You can cast commune with nature as a ritual.

features.name: Totemic Attunement
features.description: You gain a magical benefit based on a totem animal. Options: Bear (hostile creatures within 5 feet have disadvantage on attacks against others), Eagle (flying speed equal to walking speed while raging, but fall if end turn in air), Wolf (bonus action to knock a Large or smaller creature prone when you hit it with melee attack).

features.name: Ancestral Protectors
features.description: While raging, the first creature you hit with an attack becomes the target of spectral warriors. Until the start of your next turn, that target has disadvantage on any attack roll that isn't against you, and when it hits a creature other than you with an attack, that creature has resistance to the damage dealt.

features.name: Spirit Shield
features.description: If you are raging and another creature you can see within 30 feet takes damage, you can use your reaction to reduce that damage by 2d6. This reduction increases to 3d6 at 10th level and 4d6 at 14th level.

features.name: Consult the Spirits
features.description: You can cast augury or clairvoyance, without using a spell slot or material components. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability. After you cast either spell in this way, you can't use this feature again until you finish a short or long rest.

features.name: Vengeful Ancestors
features.description: When you use your Spirit Shield to reduce damage, the attacker takes force damage equal to the damage that your Spirit Shield prevented.

features.name: Storm Aura
features.description: You emanate a stormy, magical aura while you rage. The aura extends 10 feet from you. When you enter your rage, and as a bonus action on subsequent turns, you can activate an effect based on your chosen environment (Desert, Sea, or Tundra).

features.name: Storm Soul
features.description: The storm grants you benefits even when your aura isn't active. Based on your environment: Desert (resistance to fire damage, no effects of extreme heat, set objects on fire), Sea (resistance to lightning damage, breathe underwater, swimming speed), Tundra (resistance to cold damage, no effects of extreme cold, turn water to ice).

features.name: Shielding Storm
features.description: Each creature of your choice has the damage resistance you gained from the Storm Soul feature while the creature is in your Storm Aura.

features.name: Raging Storm
features.description: The power of the storm you channel grows mightier, lashing out at your foes. Based on your environment: Desert (reaction to deal fire damage to an attacker), Sea (reaction to knock an attacker prone), Tundra (choose a creature in your aura to have its speed reduced to 0).

features.name: Divine Fury
features.description: While raging, the first creature you hit on each of your turns with a weapon attack takes extra damage equal to 1d6 + half your barbarian level. The damage is necrotic or radiant (your choice).

features.name: Warrior of the Gods
features.description: Your soul is marked for endless battle. If a spell, such as raise dead, has the sole effect of restoring you to life, the caster doesn't need material components to cast the spell on you.

features.name: Fanatical Focus
features.description: If you fail a saving throw while you're raging, you can reroll it, and you must use the new roll. You can use this ability only once per rage.

features.name: Zealous Presence
features.description: As a bonus action, you unleash a battle cry. Up to ten other creatures of your choice within 60 feet that can hear you gain advantage on attack rolls and saving throws until the start of your next turn. Once you use this, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Rage beyond Death
features.description: While you're raging, having 0 hit points doesn't knock you unconscious. You still must make death saving throws, but you don't die from them until your rage ends, and you die then only if you still have 0 hit points.

features.name: Form of the Beast
features.description: When you enter your rage, you can transform, manifesting a natural weapon. Choose Bite (1d8 piercing, regain hit points when below half HP), Claws (1d6 slashing, make an extra claw attack when you take the Attack action), or Tail (1d8 piercing, reach, use reaction to add to AC against an attack).

features.name: Bestial Soul
features.description: Your natural weapons count as magical for overcoming resistance. When you finish a short or long rest, choose one benefit that lasts until your next rest: a swimming speed and underwater breathing, a climbing speed and ability to climb difficult surfaces, or the ability to extend your jump distance with an Athletics check.

features.name: Infectious Fury
features.description: When you hit a creature with your natural weapons while raging, you can curse it. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or suffer one of two effects: it must use its reaction to make a melee attack against another creature, or it takes 2d12 psychic damage. You can use this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all uses after a long rest.

features.name: Call the Hunt
features.description: When you enter your rage, you can choose a number of other willing creatures you can see equal to your Constitution modifier. You gain 5 temporary hit points for each, and until the rage ends, they can each add a d6 to the damage of one attack on each of their turns. You can use this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all uses after a long rest.

features.name: Magic Awareness
features.description: As an action, you can open your awareness to the presence of concentrated magic. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any spell or magic item within 60 feet not behind total cover. You can use this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all uses after a long rest.

features.name: Wild Surge
features.description: When you enter your rage, roll on the Wild Magic table to determine a magical effect produced.

features.name: Bolstering Magic
features.description: As an action, you can touch one creature to confer a benefit: for 10 minutes, the creature can roll a d3 and add it to an attack roll or ability check; or you roll a d3 and the creature regains one expended spell slot of a level equal to the number rolled or lower. You can use this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all uses after a long rest.

features.name: Unstable Backlash
features.description: When you take damage or fail a saving throw while raging, you can use your reaction to roll on the Wild Magic table and immediately produce the effect. This effect replaces your current Wild Magic effect.

features.name: Controlled Surge
features.description: Whenever you roll on the Wild Magic table, you can roll the die twice and choose which effect to unleash. If you roll the same number on both dice, you can ignore the number and choose any effect on the table.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: You gain proficiency with three skills of your choice.

features.name: Cutting Words
features.description: When a creature you can see within 60 feet makes an attack roll, ability check, or damage roll, you can use your reaction to expend one Bardic Inspiration die, rolling it and subtracting the number from the creature's roll. You can do so after the roll but before the outcome.

features.name: Additional Magical Secrets
features.description: You learn two spells of your choice from any class. They count as bard spells.

features.name: Peerless Skill
features.description: When you make an ability check, you can expend one Bardic Inspiration die and add it to your roll. You can do so after rolling but before knowing success or failure.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: You gain proficiency with medium armor, shields, and martial weapons.

features.name: Combat Inspiration
features.description: A creature with a Bardic Inspiration die from you can roll it and add it to a weapon damage roll, or use its reaction to add it to AC against an attack.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action.

features.name: Battle Magic
features.description: When you use your action to cast a bard spell, you can make one weapon attack as a bonus action.

features.name: Mantle of Inspiration
features.description: As a bonus action, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration to grant yourself a wondrous appearance. Choose creatures you can see within 60 feet, up to your Charisma modifier. They gain temporary hit points and can use their reaction to move up to their speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

features.name: Enthralling Performance
features.description: If you perform for at least 1 minute, you can attempt to inspire wonder in your audience. Choose a number of humanoids within 60 feet who watched and listened, up to your Charisma modifier. Each must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed for 1 hour. Once used, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

features.name: Mantle of Majesty
features.description: As a bonus action, you cast command without expending a spell slot and take on an appearance of unearthly beauty for 1 minute or until your concentration ends. During this time, you can cast command as a bonus action on each of your turns without expending a spell slot. Any creature charmed by you automatically fails its saving throw against this command. Once used, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Unbreakable Majesty
features.description: Your appearance permanently gains an otherworldly aspect. As a bonus action, you can assume a magically majestic presence for 1 minute. For the duration, whenever a creature tries to attack you for the first time on a turn, the attacker must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, it can't attack you on this turn and must choose a new target. Once you assume this presence, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: You gain proficiency with medium armor and the scimitar. If you're proficient with a simple or martial melee weapon, you can use it as a spellcasting focus for your bard spells.

features.name: Fighting Style
features.description: You adopt a style of fighting. Choose either Dueling ( +2 damage when wielding a melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons) or Two-Weapon Fighting (add your ability modifier to the damage of the second attack).

features.name: Blade Flourish
features.description: Whenever you take the Attack action, your walking speed increases by 10 feet until the end of the turn. If a weapon attack hits a creature, you can expend one Bardic Inspiration die to use one of the following: Defensive Flourish (add the die to damage and to your AC until your next turn), Slashing Flourish (add the die to damage against the target and another creature you can see within 5 feet), or Mobile Flourish (add the die to damage, push the target up to 5 feet + the number rolled, then use your reaction to move up to your speed to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the target).

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Master's Flourish
features.description: Whenever you use a Blade Flourish option, you can roll a d6 and use it instead of expending a Bardic Inspiration die.

features.name: Psychic Blades
features.description: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration to deal an extra 2d6 psychic damage. You can do so only once per round on your turn. The psychic damage increases to 3d6 at 5th level, 5d6 at 10th level, and 8d6 at 15th level.

features.name: Words of Terror
features.description: If you speak to a humanoid alone for at least 1 minute, you can attempt to seed paranoia. At the end of the conversation, the target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be frightened of you or another creature of your choice for 1 hour. Once used, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

features.name: Mantle of Whispers
features.description: When a humanoid dies within 30 feet, you can capture its shadow using your reaction. You retain this shadow until you use it. As an action, you can use the shadow to magically transform into a disguise, looking like the dead person for 1 hour. You gain access to general information about its background and personal life. Another creature can see through this disguise with a contested Wisdom (Insight) check against your Charisma (Deception) check, which you make with a +5 bonus.

features.name: Shadow Lore
features.description: As an action, you magically whisper a phrase that only one creature within 30 feet can hear. It must make a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by you for 8 hours. It interprets the whispers as a description of its most mortifying secret and obeys your commands for fear you will reveal it. It won't risk its life for you unless it was already inclined to do so. Once used, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Mote of Potential
features.description: Whenever you give a creature a Bardic Inspiration die, you can create a Tiny mote of potential that orbits it. When the creature uses the die, it provides an additional effect based on whether it was for an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw.

features.name: Performance of Creation
features.description: As an action, you can channel magic to create one nonmagical item of your choice in an unoccupied space within 10 feet. The item's gp value can't be more than 20 times your bard level, and it must be Medium or smaller. It disappears after a number of hours equal to your proficiency bonus. The size increases to Large at 6th level and Huge at 14th level.

features.name: Animating Performance
features.description: As an action, you can target a Large or smaller nonmagical item within 30 feet and animate it. The animate item uses the Dancing Item stat block. It lives for 1 hour, until reduced to 0 hit points, or until you die. In combat, it shares your initiative count. You can command it with a bonus action.

features.name: Creative Crescendo
features.description: When you use Performance of Creation, you can create more than one item at once, up to your Charisma modifier. Only one can be of the maximum size you can create. You are no longer limited by gp value when creating items with Performance of Creation.

features.name: Silver Tongue
features.description: When you make a Charisma (Persuasion) or Charisma (Deception) check, you can treat a d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10.

features.name: Unsettling Words
features.description: As a bonus action, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration and choose one creature within 60 feet. Roll the Bardic Inspiration die. The creature must subtract the number rolled from the next saving throw it makes before the start of your next turn.

features.name: Unfailing Inspiration
features.description: When a creature adds one of your Bardic Inspiration dice to an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw and the roll fails, the creature can keep the Bardic Inspiration die.

features.name: Universal Speech
features.description: As an action, choose one or more creatures within 60 feet, up to your Charisma modifier. They can magically understand you, regardless of the language you speak, for 1 hour. Once used, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest, unless you expend a spell slot to use it again.

features.name: Infectious Inspiration
features.description: When a creature within 60 feet adds one of your Bardic Inspiration dice to a roll and the roll succeeds, you can use your reaction to give a different creature (other than yourself) a Bardic Inspiration die without expending any uses. You can use this reaction a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier, regaining all uses after a long rest.

features.name: Blessings of Knowledge
features.description: You learn two languages of your choice. You become proficient in two of the following skills: Arcana, History, Nature, or Religion. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check using those skills.

features.name: Domain Spells
features.description: You gain domain spells: command, identify at 1st; augury, suggestion at 3rd; nondetection, speak with dead at 5th; arcane eye, confusion at 7th; legend lore, scrying at 9th.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Knowledge of the Ages
features.description: As an action, you choose one skill or tool. For 10 minutes, you have proficiency with it.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Read Thoughts
features.description: As an action, choose a creature within 60 feet. It must make a Wisdom save. On failure, you can read its surface thoughts for 1 minute. During that time, you can use your action to end this effect and cast suggestion on it without expending a spell slot, and it automatically fails its save.

features.name: Potent Spellcasting
features.description: You add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any cleric cantrip.

features.name: Visions of the Past
features.description: You can spend at least 1 minute meditating to receive visions of recent events related to an object you hold or your immediate surroundings. You can meditate for up to your Wisdom score minutes, and you learn about significant events. Once used, you must finish a short or long rest before using again.

features.name: Bonus Proficiency
features.description: You gain proficiency with heavy armor.

features.name: Disciple of Life
features.description: Your healing spells are more effective. Whenever you use a spell of 1st level or higher to restore hit points, the creature regains additional hit points equal to 2 + the spell's level.

features.name: Domain Spells
features.description: You gain domain spells: bless, cure wounds at 1st; lesser restoration, spiritual weapon at 3rd; beacon of hope, revivify at 5th; death ward, guardian of faith at 7th; mass cure wounds, raise dead at 9th.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Preserve Life
features.description: As an action, you present your holy symbol and evoke healing energy that can restore a number of hit points equal to five times your cleric level. Choose any creatures within 30 feet, and divide those hit points among them. This can restore a creature to no more than half its hit point maximum. Can't be used on undead or constructs.

features.name: Blessed Healer
features.description: When you cast a spell of 1st level or higher that restores hit points to a creature other than you, you regain hit points equal to 2 + the spell's level.

features.name: Divine Strike
features.description: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 radiant damage. At 14th level, the extra damage increases to 2d8.

features.name: Supreme Healing
features.description: When you would normally roll one or more dice to restore hit points with a spell, you instead use the highest number possible for each die.

features.name: Bonus Cantrip
features.description: You gain the light cantrip if you don't already know it.

features.name: Warding Flare
features.description: When you are attacked by a creature within 30 feet that you can see, you can use your reaction to impose disadvantage on the attack roll. You can use this a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier, regains after long rest.

features.name: Domain Spells
features.description: You gain domain spells: burning hands, faerie fire at 1st; flaming sphere, scorching ray at 3rd; daylight, fireball at 5th; guardian of faith, wall of fire at 7th; flame strike, scrying at 9th.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Radiance of the Dawn
features.description: As an action, you present your holy symbol, dispelling any magical darkness within 30 feet. Each hostile creature within 30 feet must make a Constitution save, taking 2d10 + cleric level radiant damage on a failed save, or half on a success.

features.name: Improved Flare
features.description: You can also use your Warding Flare when a creature within 30 feet attacks a creature other than you.

features.name: Potent Spellcasting
features.description: You add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any cleric cantrip.

features.name: Corona of Light
features.description: You can use your action to activate an aura of sunlight for 1 minute. You emit bright light in a 60-foot radius and dim light 30 feet beyond. Enemies in the bright light have disadvantage on saving throws against spells that deal fire or radiant damage.

features.name: Acolyte of Nature
features.description: You learn one druid cantrip of your choice. You also gain proficiency in one of the following skills: Animal Handling, Nature, or Survival.

features.name: Bonus Proficiency
features.description: You gain proficiency with heavy armor.

features.name: Domain Spells
features.description: You gain domain spells: animal friendship, speak with animals at 1st; barkskin, spike growth at 3rd; plant growth, wind wall at 5th; dominate beast, grasping vine at 7th; insect plague, tree stride at 9th.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Charm Animals and Plants
features.description: As an action, you present your holy symbol. Each beast or plant creature within 30 feet that can see you must make a Wisdom save or be charmed for 1 minute or until it takes damage.

features.name: Dampen Elements
features.description: When you or a creature within 30 feet takes acid, cold, fire, lightning, or thunder damage, you can use your reaction to grant resistance to that instance of damage.

features.name: Divine Strike
features.description: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 cold, fire, or lightning damage (your choice). At 14th level, it increases to 2d8.

features.name: Master of Nature
features.description: While creatures are charmed by your Charm Animals and Plants, you can use a bonus action to verbally command what each will do on its next turn.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: You gain proficiency with martial weapons and heavy armor.

features.name: Wrath of the Storm
features.description: When a creature within 5 feet hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction to cause it to make a Dexterity save, taking 2d8 lightning or thunder damage on a failure, half on success. You can use this a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier, regains after long rest.

features.name: Domain Spells
features.description: You gain domain spells: fog cloud, thunderwave at 1st; gust of wind, shatter at 3rd; call lightning, sleet storm at 5th; control water, ice storm at 7th; destructive wave, insect plague at 9th.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Destructive Wrath
features.description: When you roll lightning or thunder damage, you can use your Channel Divinity to deal maximum damage instead of rolling.

features.name: Thunderbolt Strike
features.description: When you deal lightning damage to a Large or smaller creature, you can also push it up to 10 feet away.

features.name: Divine Strike
features.description: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 thunder damage. At 14th level, it increases to 2d8.

features.name: Stormborn
features.description: You have a flying speed equal to your current walking speed whenever you are not underground or indoors.

features.name: Blessing of the Trickster
features.description: As an action, you can touch a willing creature to give it advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks for 1 hour. You can use this feature again after you use it.

features.name: Domain Spells
features.description: You gain domain spells: charm person, disguise self at 1st; mirror image, pass without trace at 3rd; blink, dispel magic at 5th; dimension door, polymorph at 7th; dominate person, modify memory at 9th.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Invoke Duplicity
features.description: As an action, you create an illusory duplicate of yourself that lasts for 1 minute or until you lose concentration. The illusion appears within 30 feet. As a bonus action, you can move it up to 30 feet. For the duration, you can cast spells as though you were in the illusion's space. When both you and your illusion are within 5 feet of a creature, you have advantage on attack rolls against it.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Cloak of Shadows
features.description: As an action, you become invisible until the end of your next turn. You become visible if you attack or cast a spell.

features.name: Divine Strike
features.description: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 poison damage. At 14th level, it increases to 2d8.

features.name: Improved Duplicity
features.description: You can create up to four duplicates when you use Invoke Duplicity. As a bonus action, you can move any number of them up to 30 feet.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: You gain proficiency with martial weapons and heavy armor.

features.name: War Priest
features.description: When you use the Attack action, you can make one weapon attack as a bonus action. You can use this a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier, regains after long rest.

features.name: Domain Spells
features.description: You gain domain spells: divine favor, shield of faith at 1st; magic weapon, spiritual weapon at 3rd; crusader's mantle, spirit guardians at 5th; freedom of movement, stoneskin at 7th; flame strike, hold monster at 9th.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Guided Strike
features.description: When you make an attack roll, you can use your Channel Divinity to gain a +10 bonus to the roll after seeing the roll.

features.name: Channel Divinity: War God's Blessing
features.description: When a creature within 30 feet makes an attack roll, you can use your reaction to grant it a +10 bonus to the roll after seeing the roll.

features.name: Divine Strike
features.description: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 damage of the same type as the weapon. At 14th level, it increases to 2d8.

features.name: Avatar of Battle
features.description: You gain resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical weapons.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: You gain proficiency with heavy armor and smith's tools.

features.name: Blessing of the Forge
features.description: At the end of a long rest, you can touch one nonmagical object that is a suit of armor or a simple or martial weapon. Until the end of your next long rest or until you die, the object becomes a magic item, granting a +1 bonus to AC if it's armor or a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls if it's a weapon. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Domain Spells
features.description: You gain domain spells: searing smite, shield of faith at 1st; branding smite, heat metal at 3rd; elemental weapon, protection from energy at 5th; fabricate, wall of fire at 7th; animate objects, creation at 9th.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Artisan's Blessing
features.description: You conduct an hour-long ritual that crafts a nonmagical item that must include some metal, worth no more than 100 gp. As part of the ritual, you must lay out metal, which can include coins, with a value equal to the creation. The metal is consumed and transforms into the creation.

features.name: Soul of the Forge
features.description: You gain resistance to fire damage. While wearing heavy armor, you gain a +1 bonus to AC.

features.name: Divine Strike
features.description: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 fire damage to the target. When you reach 14th level, the extra damage increases to 2d8.

features.name: Saint of Forge and Fire
features.description: You gain immunity to fire damage. While wearing heavy armor, you have resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks.

features.name: Domain Spells
features.description: You gain domain spells: bane, false life at 1st; gentle repose, ray of enfeeblement at 3rd; revivify, vampiric touch at 5th; blight, death ward at 7th; antilife shell, raise dead at 9th.

features.name: Circle of Mortality
features.description: When you would normally roll one or more dice to restore hit points with a spell to a creature at 0 hit points, you instead use the highest number possible for each die. You learn the spare the dying cantrip, which has a range of 30 feet and can be cast as a bonus action.

features.name: Eyes of the Grave
features.description: As an action, you can open your awareness to magically detect undead. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any undead within 60 feet not behind total cover and not protected from divination magic. You can use this a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier, regaining all uses after a long rest.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Path to the Grave
features.description: As an action, you choose one creature you can see within 30 feet, cursing it until the end of your next turn. The next time you or an ally hits the cursed creature with an attack, the creature has vulnerability to all of that attack's damage, and then the curse ends.

features.name: Sentinel at Death's Door
features.description: As a reaction when you or a creature you can see within 30 feet suffers a critical hit, you can turn that hit into a normal hit. You can use this a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier, regaining all uses after a long rest.

features.name: Potent Spellcasting
features.description: You add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any cleric cantrip.

features.name: Keeper of Souls
features.description: When an enemy you can see dies within 60 feet, you or one creature of your choice within 60 feet regains hit points equal to the enemy's number of Hit Dice. You can use this feature only if you aren't incapacitated. Once you use it, you can't do so again until the start of your next turn.

features.name: Domain Spells
features.description: You gain domain spells: command, heroism at 1st; hold person, zone of truth at 3rd; mass healing word, slow at 5th; compulsion, locate creature at 7th; commune, dominate person at 9th.

features.name: Voice of Authority
features.description: You can invoke the power of law to drive an ally to attack. If you cast a spell with a spell slot of 1st level or higher and target an ally with the spell, that ally can use their reaction immediately after the spell to make one weapon attack against a creature of your choice that you can see.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Order's Demand
features.description: As an action, you present your holy symbol and force each creature of your choice within 30 feet that can see you to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a creature is charmed by you until the end of your next turn or until it takes damage. While charmed, it can't take reactions and has disadvantage on attack rolls.

features.name: Embodiment of the Law
features.description: If you cast a spell of the enchantment school using a spell slot of 1st level or higher, you can change the spell's casting time to 1 bonus action for this casting, provided the spell's casting time is normally 1 action. You can use this a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier, regaining all uses after a long rest.

features.name: Divine Strike
features.description: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 psychic damage. At 14th level, the extra damage increases to 2d8.

features.name: Order's Wrath
features.description: If you deal your Divine Strike damage to a creature on your turn, you can curse that creature until the start of your next turn. The next time one of your allies hits the cursed creature with an attack, the target also takes 2d8 psychic damage, and the curse ends. You can curse a creature in this way only once per turn.

features.name: Domain Spells
features.description: You gain domain spells: heroism, sanctuary at 1st; aid, warding bond at 3rd; beacon of hope, sending at 5th; aura of purity, Otiluke's resilient sphere at 7th; greater restoration, Rary's telepathic bond at 9th.

features.name: Implement of Peace
features.description: You gain proficiency in the Insight, Performance, or Persuasion skill (your choice).

features.name: Emboldening Bond
features.description: As an action, you choose a number of willing creatures within 30 feet equal to your proficiency bonus. You create a magical bond among them for 10 minutes. While any bonded creature is within 30 feet of another, it can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw it makes. Each creature can add the d4 no more than once per turn.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Balm of Peace
features.description: As an action, you can move up to your speed, without provoking opportunity attacks, and when you move within 5 feet of any other creature during this action, you can restore hit points to that creature equal to 2d6 + your Wisdom modifier. A creature can receive this healing only once whenever you take this action.

features.name: Protective Bond
features.description: When a creature affected by your Emboldening Bond is about to take damage, a second bonded creature within 30 feet can use its reaction to teleport within 5 feet of the first and take all the damage instead.

features.name: Potent Spellcasting
features.description: You add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any cleric cantrip.

features.name: Expansive Bond
features.description: The benefits of your Emboldening Bond and Protective Bond now work when the creatures are within 60 feet of each other. Moreover, when a creature uses Protective Bond to take someone else's damage, it has resistance to that damage.

features.name: Domain Spells
features.description: You gain domain spells: faerie fire, sleep at 1st; moonbeam, see invisibility at 3rd; aura of vitality, Leomund's tiny hut at 5th; aura of life, greater invisibility at 7th; circle of power, mislead at 9th.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: You gain proficiency with martial weapons and heavy armor.

features.name: Eyes of Night
features.description: You have darkvision out to a range of 300 feet. As an action, you can share this darkvision with willing creatures you can see within 10 feet, up to your Wisdom modifier, for 1 hour. Once you share it, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest, unless you expend a spell slot to share it again.

features.name: Vigilant Blessing
features.description: As an action, you give one creature you touch advantage on the next initiative roll it makes. This benefit ends immediately after the roll or if you use this feature again.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Twilight Sanctuary
features.description: As an action, you present your holy symbol, and a 30-foot radius sphere of twilight emanates from you, filled with dim light. It lasts for 1 minute. Whenever a creature (including you) ends its turn in the sphere, you can grant it temporary hit points equal to 1d6 plus your cleric level, or end one effect on it causing it to be charmed or frightened.

features.name: Steps of Night
features.description: As a bonus action when you are in dim light or darkness, you can give yourself a flying speed equal to your walking speed for 1 minute. You can use this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all uses after a long rest.

features.name: Divine Strike
features.description: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 radiant damage. When you reach 14th level, the extra damage increases to 2d8.

features.name: Twilight Shroud
features.description: You and your allies have half cover while in the sphere created by your Twilight Sanctuary.

features.name: Bonus Cantrip
features.description: You learn one additional druid cantrip of your choice.

features.name: Natural Recovery
features.description: During a short rest, you can recover expended spell slots with a combined level equal to or less than half your druid level (rounded up), and none of the slots can be 6th level or higher. You can't use this again until after a long rest.

features.name: Circle Spells
features.description: You gain access to circle spells based on the land you chose: arctic, coast, desert, forest, grassland, mountain, swamp, or Underdark. You always have them prepared.

features.name: Land's Stride
features.description: Moving through nonmagical difficult terrain costs you no extra movement. You can pass through nonmagical plants without being slowed or taking damage. You have advantage on saving throws against plants that are magically created or manipulated to impede movement.

features.name: Nature's Ward
features.description: You can't be charmed or frightened by elementals or fey, and you are immune to poison and disease.

features.name: Nature's Sanctuary
features.description: When a beast or plant creature attacks you, it must make a Wisdom save against your druid spell save DC. On a failure, it must choose a different target, or the attack misses. On a success, it is immune for 24 hours.

features.name: Combat Wild Shape
features.description: You can use Wild Shape on your turn as a bonus action, rather than as an action. Additionally, while transformed, you can use a bonus action to expend a spell slot to regain 1d8 hit points per level of the spell slot.

features.name: Circle Forms
features.description: You can transform into a beast with a challenge rating as high as 1 (ignoring the Max. CR column). At 6th level, you can transform into a beast with a challenge rating as high as your druid level divided by 3, rounded down.

features.name: Primal Strike
features.description: Your attacks in beast form count as magical for overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

features.name: Elemental Wild Shape
features.description: You can expend two uses of Wild Shape at the same time to transform into an air elemental, earth elemental, fire elemental, or water elemental.

features.name: Thousand Forms
features.description: You can cast alter self at will.

features.name: Balm of the Summer Court
features.description: You have a pool of fey energy represented by a number of d6s equal to your druid level. As a bonus action, you can choose a creature within 120 feet and spend a number of dice equal to half your druid level or less. The target regains hit points equal to the total, and gains 1 temporary hit point per die spent. You regain all expended dice after a long rest.

features.name: Hearth of Moonlight and Shadow
features.description: During a short or long rest, you can invoke shadowy power to guard your respite. You touch a point in space, and an invisible, 30-foot-radius sphere of magic appears, centered on that point. While within, you and your allies gain a +5 bonus to Dexterity (Stealth) and Wisdom (Perception) checks, and any light from open flames isn't visible outside it.

features.name: Hidden Paths
features.description: As a bonus action, you can teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. Alternatively, you can use your action to teleport one willing creature you touch up to 30 feet. You can use this a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier, regaining all uses after a long rest.

features.name: Walker in Dreams
features.description: When you finish a short rest, you can cast one of the following spells, without expending a spell slot: dream (with you as the messenger), scrying, or teleportation circle (opening a portal to the last location where you finished a long rest). Once you use this, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Speech of the Woods
features.description: You learn to speak, read, and write Sylvan. Beasts can understand your speech, and you can decipher their noises and motions.

features.name: Spirit Totem
features.description: As a bonus action, you can summon an incorporeal spirit to a point within 60 feet. The spirit creates a 30-foot radius aura. As a bonus action, you can move it up to 60 feet. It lasts for 1 minute. The effect depends on the spirit: Bear (gain temporary hit points, advantage on Strength checks), Hawk (grant advantage on an attack roll as a reaction, advantage on Perception checks), or Unicorn (advantage on checks to detect creatures, allies regain hit points equal to your druid level when you cast a healing spell).

features.name: Mighty Summoner
features.description: Beasts and fey that you conjure are more resilient. They appear with more hit points (2 extra per Hit Die) and their natural weapons are considered magical.

features.name: Guardian Spirit
features.description: When a beast or fey that you summoned ends its turn in your Spirit Totem aura, it regains a number of hit points equal to half your druid level.

features.name: Faithful Summons
features.description: If you are reduced to 0 hit points or are incapacitated against your will, you can immediately gain the benefits of conjure animals as if cast with a 9th-level spell slot, summoning four beasts of CR 2 or lower. The spell lasts 1 hour, requiring no concentration. Once you use this, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Circle Spells
features.description: You gain the chill touch cantrip. You gain access to additional spells: blindness/deafness, gentle repose at 3rd; animate dead, gaseous form at 5th; blight, confusion at 7th; cloudkill, contagion at 9th.

features.name: Halo of Spores
features.description: You are surrounded by invisible, necrotic spores. When a creature moves into a space within 10 feet of you or starts its turn there, you can use your reaction to deal 1d4 necrotic damage to it, unless it succeeds on a Constitution save. The damage increases to 1d6 at 6th level, 1d8 at 10th, and 1d10 at 14th.

features.name: Symbiotic Entity
features.description: As an action, you can expend a use of Wild Shape to awaken your spores, gaining 4 temporary hit points per druid level. While active, your Halo of Spores damage rolls an extra die, and your melee weapon attacks deal an extra 1d6 necrotic damage. It lasts 10 minutes, until you lose the temp HP, or until you use Wild Shape again.

features.name: Fungal Infestation
features.description: If a beast or humanoid that is Small or Medium dies within 10 feet of you, you can use your reaction to animate it as a zombie for 1 hour. The zombie obeys your mental commands. You can use this a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier, regaining all uses after a long rest.

features.name: Spreading Spores
features.description: As a bonus action while Symbiotic Entity is active, you can hurl spores up to 30 feet, creating a 10-foot cube of spores for 1 minute. Whenever a creature moves into the cube or starts its turn there, it takes your Halo of Spores damage. While the cube persists, you can't use your Halo of Spores reaction.

features.name: Fungal Body
features.description: You can't be blinded, deafened, frightened, or poisoned, and any critical hit against you counts as a normal hit unless you're incapacitated.

features.name: Star Map
features.description: You've created a star chart that can serve as a spellcasting focus. You know the guidance cantrip, have guiding bolt prepared, and can cast guiding bolt without a spell slot a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all uses after a long rest.

features.name: Starry Form
features.description: As a bonus action, you can expend a use of Wild Shape to take on a starry form for 10 minutes. You shed bright light in 10 feet and dim light for an additional 10 feet. Choose a constellation: Archer (make a ranged spell attack as a bonus action, dealing 1d8 + Wisdom radiant damage), Chalice (when you cast a healing spell, you or another within 30 feet regain 1d8 + Wisdom hit points), or Dragon (when making an Int/Wis check or Con save for concentration, treat a d20 roll of 9 or lower as 10).

features.name: Cosmic Omen
features.description: When you finish a long rest, you can consult your Star Map for omens. Roll a die. Until your next long rest, you have a reaction based on whether you rolled even or odd. Weal (even): you can use a reaction to add a d6 to an attack, save, or check. Woe (odd): you can use a reaction to subtract a d6 from an attack, save, or check. You can use this reaction a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus.

features.name: Twinkling Constellations
features.description: The 1d8 of the Archer and the Chalice becomes 2d8. While the Dragon is active, you have a flying speed of 20 feet and can hover. At the start of each of your turns while in Starry Form, you can change which constellation glimmers on your body.

features.name: Full of Stars
features.description: While in your Starry Form, you become partially incorporeal, giving you resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage.

features.name: Circle Spells
features.description: You gain access to additional spells: burning hands, cure wounds at 2nd; flaming sphere, scorching ray at 3rd; plant growth, revivify at 5th; aura of life, fire shield at 7th; flame strike, mass cure wounds at 9th.

features.name: Summon Wildfire Spirit
features.description: As an action, you can expend one use of Wild Shape to summon your wildfire spirit. The spirit appears within 30 feet. Each creature within 10 feet of it must make a Dexterity save or take 2d6 fire damage. The spirit is friendly and obeys commands. In combat, it shares your initiative count.

features.name: Enhanced Bond
features.description: Whenever you cast a spell that deals fire damage or restores hit points while your wildfire spirit is summoned, roll a d8 and gain a bonus to one damage or healing roll of the spell. When you cast a spell with a range other than self, it can originate from you or your spirit.

features.name: Cauterizing Flames
features.description: When a Small or larger creature dies within 30 feet of you or your spirit, a harmless spectral flame appears in its space for 1 minute. When a creature you can see enters that space, you can use your reaction to extinguish the flame and heal the creature or deal 2d10 + Wisdom fire damage. You can use this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus.

features.name: Blazing Revival
features.description: If your spirit is within 120 feet when you are reduced to 0 hit points, you can cause the spirit to drop to 0 hit points. You then regain half your hit points and immediately rise to your feet. Once used, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Improved Critical
features.description: Your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20.

features.name: Remarkable Athlete
features.description: You can add half your proficiency bonus (round up) to any Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution check that doesn't already use your proficiency bonus. Also, when you make a running long jump, the distance you can cover increases by a number of feet equal to your Strength modifier.

features.name: Additional Fighting Style
features.description: You can choose a second Fighting Style option.

features.name: Superior Critical
features.description: Your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 18-20.

features.name: Survivor
features.description: At the start of each of your turns, you regain hit points equal to 5 + your Constitution modifier if you have no more than half of your hit points left. You don't gain this benefit if you have 0 hit points.

features.name: Combat Superiority
features.description: You learn maneuvers that are fueled by superiority dice. You have four superiority dice (d8s) and learn three maneuvers. You regain expended dice after a short or long rest. You learn additional maneuvers at 7th, 10th, and 15th levels, and gain another die at 7th and 15th.

features.name: Student of War
features.description: You gain proficiency with one type of artisan's tools of your choice.

features.name: Know Your Enemy
features.description: If you spend at least 1 minute observing or interacting with another creature outside combat, you can learn certain information about its capabilities compared to your own. The DM tells you if it is equal, superior, or inferior in two characteristics of your choice: Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Armor Class, current hit points, total class levels, or fighter class levels.

features.name: Improved Combat Superiority
features.description: Your superiority dice become d10s.

features.name: Relentless
features.description: When you roll initiative and have no superiority dice remaining, you regain 1 superiority die.

features.name: Improved Combat Superiority
features.description: Your superiority dice become d12s.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You learn wizard spells from the abjuration and evocation schools. You have cantrips and spell slots as shown in the Eldritch Knight Spellcasting table.

features.name: Weapon Bond
features.description: You can bond with one weapon over a 1-hour ritual. You can't be disarmed of that weapon unless incapacitated, and you can summon it to your hand as a bonus action if it's on the same plane. You can have up to two bonded weapons.

features.name: War Magic
features.description: When you use your action to cast a cantrip, you can make one weapon attack as a bonus action.

features.name: Eldritch Strike
features.description: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, that creature has disadvantage on the next saving throw it makes against a spell you cast before the end of your next turn.

features.name: Arcane Charge
features.description: When you use your Action Surge, you can teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see before or after the additional action.

features.name: Improved War Magic
features.description: When you use your action to cast a spell, you can make one weapon attack as a bonus action.

features.name: Arcane Archer Lore
features.description: You gain proficiency in the Arcana or Nature skill.

features.name: Arcane Shot
features.description: You learn two Arcane Shot options of your choice. When you fire an arrow from a shortbow or longbow, you can apply one Arcane Shot option, expending one use per shot. You have two uses per short or long rest. Your save DC = 8 + proficiency + Intelligence.

features.name: Magic Arrow
features.description: Your arrows are considered magical for overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

features.name: Curving Shot
features.description: If you make an attack roll with a magic arrow and miss, you can use a bonus action to reroll the attack roll against a different target within 60 feet.

features.name: Ever- Ready Shot
features.description: Whenever you roll initiative and have no uses of Arcane Shot remaining, you regain one use.

features.name: Bonus Proficiency
features.description: You gain proficiency in one of the following skills: Animal Handling, History, Insight, Performance, or Persuasion. Alternatively, you learn one language of your choice.

features.name: Born to the Saddle
features.description: You have advantage on saves to avoid falling off your mount. If you fall off and descend no more than 10 feet, you can land on your feet. Mounting or dismounting costs only 5 feet of movement.

features.name: Unwavering Mark
features.description: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can mark it until the end of your next turn. It has disadvantage on attacks that don't target you. If it deals damage to anyone else, you can make a special bonus action attack against it with advantage, dealing extra damage equal to half your fighter level. You can do this a number of times equal to your Strength modifier.

features.name: Warding Maneuver
features.description: If you or a creature within 5 feet is hit by an attack, you can roll 1d8 as a reaction if you're wielding a melee weapon or shield. Add the number rolled to the target's AC against that attack. If it still hits, the target has resistance against that attack's damage. You can use this a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier.

features.name: Hold the Line
features.description: Creatures provoke an opportunity attack from you when they move 5 feet or more while within your reach. If you hit with an opportunity attack, the target's speed is reduced to 0 until the end of the turn.

features.name: Ferocious Charger
features.description: If you move at least 10 feet in a straight line right before attacking and you hit, the target must succeed on a Strength save or be knocked prone. You can use this only once on each of your turns.

features.name: Vigilant Defender
features.description: In combat, you get a special reaction that you can take once on every creature's turn, except your own. You can use this special reaction only to make an opportunity attack.

features.name: Bonus Proficiency
features.description: You gain proficiency in one of the following skills: History, Insight, Performance, or Persuasion. Alternatively, you learn one language of your choice.

features.name: Fighting Spirit
features.description: As a bonus action, you can give yourself advantage on weapon attack rolls until the end of the current turn. You also gain temporary hit points equal to 5. You can use this three times, regaining all uses after a long rest. Temporary HP increase to 10 at 10th level and 15 at 15th level.

features.name: Elegant Courtier
features.description: You gain a bonus to Charisma (Persuasion) checks equal to your Wisdom modifier. You also gain proficiency in Wisdom saving throws. If you already have this proficiency, you instead gain proficiency in Intelligence or Charisma saves.

features.name: Tireless Spirit
features.description: When you roll initiative and have no uses of Fighting Spirit remaining, you regain one use.

features.name: Rapid Strike
features.description: If you take the Attack action and have advantage on an attack roll against one of the targets, you can forgo the advantage for that roll to make an additional weapon attack against that target, as part of the same action. You can do so no more than once per turn.

features.name: Strength before Death
features.description: If you take damage that reduces you to 0 hit points and doesn't kill you outright, you can use your reaction to delay falling unconscious and immediately take an extra turn. During that turn, you still make death saves as normal. When the extra turn ends, you fall unconscious if you still have 0 hit points. Once used, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Psionic Power
features.description: You have Psionic Energy dice equal to twice your proficiency bonus (d6s). They fuel your powers. Regain all after a long rest. As a bonus action, you can regain one die after a short or long rest. Powers: Protective Field (reaction to reduce damage), Psionic Strike (deal extra force damage after a hit), Telekinetic Movement (move an object or willing creature).

features.name: Telekinetic Adept
features.description: You gain new ways to use your telekinetic abilities: Psi-Powered Leap (bonus action to gain flying speed equal to twice walking speed) and Telekinetic Thrust (when you deal Psionic Strike damage, you can knock the target prone or move it up to 10 feet).

features.name: Guarded Mind
features.description: You have resistance to psychic damage. If you start your turn charmed or frightened, you can expend a Psionic Energy die to end every effect subjecting you to those conditions.

features.name: Bulwark of Force
features.description: As a bonus action, you can choose creatures you can see within 30 feet, up to your Intelligence modifier. They are protected by half cover for 1 minute. Once used, you can't do so again until after a long rest, unless you expend a die to do it again.

features.name: Telekinetic Master
features.description: You can cast telekinesis, requiring no components, using Intelligence as your spellcasting ability. While concentrating on it, you can make one weapon attack as a bonus action on each of your turns.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: You gain proficiency with smith's tools, and you learn to speak, read, and write Giant.

features.name: Rune Carver
features.description: You learn two runes of your choice from among Cloud, Fire, Frost, or Stone. At certain levels, you learn more. When you finish a long rest, you can touch a number of objects equal to the number of runes you know and inscribe a different rune onto each. You can use the runes' properties.

features.name: Giant's Might
features.description: As a bonus action, you gain benefits for 1 minute: you become Large if you can, you have advantage on Strength checks and saves, and once per turn one attack deals an extra 1d6 damage. You can use this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus.

features.name: Runic Shield
features.description: When another creature you can see within 60 feet is hit by an attack, you can use your reaction to force the attacker to reroll the d20. You can use this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus.

features.name: Great Stature
features.description: You grow 3d4 inches in height. The extra damage from Giant's Might increases to 1d8.

features.name: Master of Runes
features.description: You can invoke each rune you know from Rune Carver twice, rather than once, and you regain all uses after a short or long rest.

features.name: Runic Juggernaut
features.description: The extra damage from Giant's Might increases to 1d10. When you use it, your size can increase to Huge, and your reach increases by 5 feet while you are that size.

features.name: Open Hand Technique
features.description: Whenever you hit a creature with one of the attacks granted by your Flurry of Blows, you can impose one of the following effects: it must succeed on a Dexterity save or be knocked prone; it must make a Strength save or be pushed up to 15 feet away; or it can't take reactions until the end of your next turn.

features.name: Wholeness of Body
features.description: As an action, you can regain hit points equal to three times your monk level. Once used, you must finish a long rest before using again.

features.name: Tranquility
features.description: At the end of a long rest, you gain the effect of a sanctuary spell that lasts until the start of your next long rest (save DC = 8 + Wisdom + proficiency).

features.name: Quivering Palm
features.description: When you hit a creature with an unarmed strike, you can spend 3 ki points to start vibrations that last for a number of days equal to your monk level. The vibrations are harmless unless you use your action to end them. The target must make a Constitution save or be reduced to 0 hit points; on success, it takes 10d10 necrotic damage. You can have only one creature under this effect at a time.

features.name: Shadow Arts
features.description: You can use your ki to duplicate the effects of certain spells. As an action, you can spend 2 ki points to cast darkness, darkvision, pass without trace, or silence, without material components. You also gain the minor illusion cantrip.

features.name: Shadow Step
features.description: When you are in dim light or darkness, as a bonus action you can teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space also in dim light or darkness. You then have advantage on the first melee attack you make before the end of the turn.

features.name: Cloak of Shadows
features.description: When you are in dim light or darkness, you can use your action to become invisible. You remain invisible until you attack, cast a spell, or are in bright light.

features.name: Opportunist
features.description: Whenever a creature within 5 feet of you is hit by an attack made by another creature, you can use your reaction to make a melee attack against that creature.

features.name: Disciple of the Elements
features.description: You learn magical disciplines that harness the power of the four elements. You know Elemental Attunement and one other elemental discipline of your choice. You learn another at 6th, 11th, and 17th levels. Each discipline requires you to spend ki points. Some allow you to cast spells; you can spend additional ki to increase the spell's level.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: You gain proficiency in the Performance skill and with brewer's supplies.

features.name: Drunken Technique
features.description: Whenever you use Flurry of Blows, you gain the benefit of the Disengage action, and your walking speed increases by 10 feet until the end of the turn.

features.name: Tipsy Sway
features.description: You gain benefits: Leap to Your Feet (standing up from prone costs only 5 feet of movement) and Redirect Attack (when a creature misses you with a melee attack, you can spend 1 ki point as a reaction to cause that attack to hit another creature within 5 feet).

features.name: Drunkard's Luck
features.description: When you make an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw with disadvantage, you can spend 2 ki points to cancel the disadvantage for that roll.

features.name: Intoxicated Frenzy
features.description: When you use Flurry of Blows, you can make up to three additional attacks with it (up to a total of five Flurry of Blows attacks), provided that each Flurry of Blows attack targets a different creature this turn.

features.name: Path of the Kensei
features.description: You choose two types of weapons to be your kensei weapons (one melee, one ranged, lacking heavy and special properties; longbow is valid). They are monk weapons for you. You gain: Agile Parry (if you make an unarmed strike as part of the Attack action while holding a kensei melee weapon, you gain +2 AC until your next turn), Kensei's Shot (bonus action to make ranged attacks with a kensei weapon deal an extra 1d4 damage), and Way of the Brush (proficiency with calligrapher's or painter's supplies).

features.name: One with the Blade
features.description: You gain benefits: your kensei weapon attacks count as magical for overcoming resistance and immunity, and when you hit with a kensei weapon on your turn, you can spend 1 ki point to deal an extra Martial Arts die of damage.

features.name: Sharpen the Blade
features.description: As a bonus action, you can spend up to 3 ki points to grant one kensei weapon you touch a +1 to attack and damage rolls for 1 minute per ki point spent, up to +3.

features.name: Unerring Accuracy
features.description: If you make an attack roll with a kensei weapon and miss, you can reroll it. You can use this feature only once on each of your turns.

features.name: Radiant Sun Bolt
features.description: You gain a new ranged attack option. As an action, you can make a special ranged attack with a range of 30 feet. It is a ranged spell attack that deals radiant damage equal to 1d4 + your Dexterity modifier. This counts as a monk weapon. When you take the Attack action, you can replace one of the attacks with this attack.

features.name: Searing Arc Strike
features.description: When you take the Attack action, you can spend 2 ki points to cause a flash of light. Each creature of your choice within 15 feet must make a Dexterity saving throw or take 3d4 fire damage.

features.name: Searing Sunburst
features.description: As an action, you can spend 2 ki points to create a burst of light. Choose a point you can see within 150 feet. Each creature within 20 feet must make a Constitution saving throw or take 2d6 radiant damage and be blinded until the start of your next turn.

features.name: Sun Shield
features.description: You gain an aura of light. As a bonus action, you can cause yourself to shed bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. While the light shines, you can use your action to fire a beam of sunlight that deals 2d12 radiant damage.

features.name: Implements of Mercy
features.description: You gain proficiency in the Insight and Medicine skills and with the herbalism kit. You also gain a special mask.

features.name: Hand of Healing
features.description: As an action, you can spend 1 ki point to touch a creature and restore hit points equal to a roll of your Martial Arts die + your Wisdom modifier. When you use Flurry of Blows, you can replace one of the unarmed strikes with a use of this feature without spending a ki point.

features.name: Hand of Harm
features.description: When you hit a creature with an unarmed strike, you can spend 1 ki point to deal extra necrotic damage equal to one roll of your Martial Arts die + your Wisdom modifier. You can use this only once per turn.

features.name: Physician's Touch
features.description: When you use Hand of Healing, you can also end one disease or condition (blinded, deafened, paralyzed, poisoned, or stunned). When you use Hand of Harm, you can also subject the target to the poisoned condition until the end of your next turn.

features.name: Flurry of Healing and Harm
features.description: When you use Flurry of Blows, you can now replace each of the unarmed strikes with a use of Hand of Healing without spending ki points. When you make an unarmed strike with Flurry of Blows, you can use Hand of Harm with that strike without spending the ki point.

features.name: Hand of Ultimate Mercy
features.description: As an action, you can touch the corpse of a creature that died within the past 24 hours and expend 5 ki points. The creature returns to life, regaining 4d10 + your Wisdom modifier hit points. It revives with conditions removed. Once used, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Arms of the Astral Self
features.description: As a bonus action, you can spend 1 ki point to summon the arms of your astral self. Each creature of your choice within 10 feet must succeed on a Dexterity save or take force damage equal to two rolls of your Martial Arts die. For 10 minutes, you gain benefits: use Wisdom for Strength checks and saves, use the arms to make unarmed strikes with reach 5 feet greater than normal, and those strikes use your Wisdom for attack/damage and deal force damage.

features.name: Visage of the Astral Self
features.description: As a bonus action, or as part of activating Arms, you can spend 1 ki point to summon the visage of your astral self for 10 minutes. While present, you gain: Astral Sight (120 ft. darkvision), Wisdom of the Spirit (advantage on Insight and Intimidation checks), and Word of the Spirit (you can direct your words to a single creature within 60 feet, or amplify your voice up to 600 feet).

features.name: Body of the Astral Self
features.description: When you have both your astral arms and visage summoned, you can cause the body to appear (no action required). While present, you gain: Deflect Energy (reaction to reduce acid, cold, fire, force, lightning, or thunder damage by 1d10 + Wisdom) and Empowered Arms (once per turn, when you hit a target with astral arms, you deal extra damage equal to your Martial Arts die).

features.name: Awakened Astral Self
features.description: As a bonus action, you can spend 5 ki points to summon the arms, visage, and body and awaken them for 10 minutes. While awakened, you gain: Armor of the Spirit (+2 bonus to AC) and Astral Barrage (when you use Extra Attack to attack twice, you can instead attack three times if all attacks are made with your astral arms).

features.name: Oath Spells
features.description: You gain oath spells: protection from evil and good, sanctuary at 3rd; lesser restoration, zone of truth at 5th; beacon of hope, dispel magic at 9th; freedom of movement, guardian of faith at 13th; commune, flame strike at 17th.

features.name: Channel Divinity
features.description: You gain two Channel Divinity options: Sacred Weapon (as an action, you can imbue one weapon you are holding with positive energy. For 1 minute, you add your Charisma modifier to attack rolls and it emits bright light; also, fiends and undead have disadvantage on attack rolls against you) and Turn the Unholy (as an action, you present your holy symbol, and each fiend or undead within 30 feet that can see you must make a Wisdom save or be turned for 1 minute or until it takes damage).

features.name: Aura of Devotion
features.description: You and friendly creatures within 10 feet can't be charmed while you are conscious. At 18th level, range increases to 30 feet.

features.name: Purity of Spirit
features.description: You are always under the effect of a protection from evil and good spell.

features.name: Holy Nimbus
features.description: As an action, you can emanate an aura of sunlight. For 1 minute, bright light shines from you in a 30-foot radius, and dim light 30 feet beyond. Enemy creatures in the bright light have disadvantage on saving throws against your spells and Channel Divinity. Also, at the start of each of your turns, each creature of your choice in the bright light takes 10 radiant damage. Once used, you must finish a long rest before using again.

features.name: Oath Spells
features.description: You gain oath spells: ensnaring strike, speak with animals at 3rd; moonbeam, misty step at 5th; plant growth, protection from energy at 9th; ice storm, stoneskin at 13th; commune with nature, tree stride at 17th.

features.name: Channel Divinity
