features.description: You gain two Channel Divinity options: Nature's Wrath (as an action, you cause spectral vines to spring up and reach for a creature within 10 feet. It must make a Strength or Dexterity save or be restrained. It repeats the save at the end of each of its turns) and Turn the Faithless (as an action, you present your holy symbol, and each fey or fiend within 30 feet that can hear you must make a Wisdom save or be turned for 1 minute or until it takes damage. If its true form is concealed, it is revealed while turned).

features.name: Aura of Warding
features.description: You and friendly creatures within 10 feet have resistance to damage from spells. At 18th level, range increases to 30 feet.

features.name: Undying Sentinel
features.description: When you are reduced to 0 hit points and not killed outright, you can choose to drop to 1 hit point instead. Once used, you must finish a long rest before using again. Also, you suffer none of the drawbacks of old age and can't be aged magically.

features.name: Elder Champion
features.description: As an action, you transform into an ancient force of nature for 1 minute. You gain: at the start of each of your turns, you regain 10 hit points; you can cast paladin spells with a casting time of 1 action as a bonus action; enemy creatures within 10 feet have disadvantage on saving throws against your spells and Channel Divinity. Once used, you must finish a long rest before using again.

features.name: Oath Spells
features.description: You gain oath spells: bane, hunter's mark at 3rd; hold person, misty step at 5th; haste, protection from energy at 9th; banishment, dimension door at 13th; hold monster, scrying at 17th.

features.name: Channel Divinity
features.description: You gain two Channel Divinity options: Abjure Enemy (as an action, you present your holy symbol and choose one creature within 60 feet. It must make a Wisdom save or be frightened for 1 minute or until it takes damage. Its speed becomes 0, and it can't take reactions) and Vow of Enmity (as a bonus action, you can utter a vow of enmity against a creature you can see within 10 feet. You have advantage on attack rolls against it for 1 minute or until it drops to 0 hit points or falls unconscious).

features.name: Relentless Avenger
features.description: Your opportunity attacks have advantage. When you hit a creature with an opportunity attack, you can move up to half your speed immediately after the attack and before the end of your turn, and this movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks.

features.name: Soul of Vengeance
features.description: When a creature under your Vow of Enmity makes an attack, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against that creature if it is within range.

features.name: Avenging Angel
features.description: As an action, you transform into an avatar of vengeance for 1 hour. You gain: wings giving you a flying speed of 60 feet; you can use your Vow of Enmity as a bonus action against each hostile creature you can see within 30 feet of you. Once used, you must finish a long rest before using again.

features.name: Oath Spells
features.description: You gain oath spells: armor of Agathys, command at 3rd; hold person, spiritual weapon at 5th; bestow curse, fear at 9th; dominate beast, stoneskin at 13th; cloudkill, dominate person at 17th.

features.name: Channel Divinity
features.description: You gain two Channel Divinity options: Conquering Presence (as an action, you force each creature of your choice within 30 feet that can see you to make a Wisdom save or be frightened for 1 minute) and Guided Strike (when you make an attack roll, you can use your Channel Divinity to gain a +10 bonus to the roll after you see the roll).

features.name: Aura of Conquest
features.description: You emanate a menacing aura while not incapacitated. If a creature is frightened of you, its speed is reduced to 0 while in the aura, and that creature takes psychic damage equal to half your paladin level if it starts its turn there.

features.name: Scornful Rebuke
features.description: Whenever a creature hits you with an attack, that creature takes psychic damage equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1) if you're not incapacitated.

features.name: Invincible Conqueror
features.description: As an action, you become an avatar of conquest for 1 minute. You have resistance to all damage, can make one additional attack as part of the Attack action, and your melee weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20. Once used, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Oath Spells
features.description: You gain oath spells: sanctuary, sleep at 3rd; calm emotions, hold person at 5th; counterspell, hypnotic pattern at 9th; Otiluke's resilient sphere, stoneskin at 13th; hold monster, wall of force at 17th.

features.name: Channel Divinity
features.description: You gain two Channel Divinity options: Emissary of Peace (as a bonus action, you grant yourself a +5 bonus to Charisma (Persuasion) checks for the next 10 minutes) and Rebuke the Violent (immediately after an attacker within 30 feet deals damage to a creature other than you, you can use your reaction to force it to make a Wisdom save, taking radiant damage equal to the damage it just dealt on a failure, or half on a success).

features.name: Aura of the Guardian
features.description: When a creature within 10 feet takes damage, you can use your reaction to take that damage instead. This doesn't transfer any other effects, and the damage can't be reduced in any way.

features.name: Protective Spirit
features.description: You regain hit points equal to 1d6 + half your paladin level if you end your turn in combat with fewer than half of your hit points remaining and you aren't incapacitated.

features.name: Emissary of Redemption
features.description: You become an avatar of peace. You have resistance to all damage dealt by other creatures. Whenever a creature hits you with an attack, it takes radiant damage equal to half the damage you take from the attack. If you attack a creature, cast a spell on it, or deal damage to it by any means but this feature, neither benefit works against that creature until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Oath Spells
features.description: You gain oath spells: guiding bolt, heroism at 3rd; enhance ability, magic weapon at 5th; haste, protection from energy at 9th; compulsion, freedom of movement at 13th; commune, flame strike at 17th.

features.name: Channel Divinity
features.description: You gain two Channel Divinity options: Peerless Athlete (as a bonus action, for 10 minutes you have advantage on Athletics and Acrobatics checks, can carry/push/drag/lift twice as much, and your jump distance increases by 10 feet) and Inspiring Smite (immediately after you deal damage with Divine Smite, you can use your Channel Divinity to grant temporary hit points to creatures of your choice within 30 feet equal to 2d8 + your level).

features.name: Aura of Alacrity
features.description: Your walking speed increases by 10 feet. The walking speed of any ally who starts their turn within 5 feet of you increases by 10 feet until the end of that turn. At 18th level, the range of the aura increases to 10 feet.

features.name: Glorious Defense
features.description: When you or another creature within 10 feet is hit by an attack roll, you can use your reaction to grant a bonus to the target's AC against that attack equal to your Charisma modifier. If the attack misses, you can make one weapon attack against the attacker. You can use this a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier.

features.name: Living Legend
features.description: As a bonus action, you gain benefits for 1 minute: advantage on all Charisma checks; once per turn when you make a weapon attack and miss, you can cause it to hit instead; if you fail a saving throw, you can use your reaction to reroll it. Once used, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest, unless you expend a 5th-level spell slot to use it again.

features.name: Oath Spells
features.description: You gain oath spells: alarm, detect magic at 3rd; moonbeam, see invisibility at 5th; counterspell, nondetection at 9th; aura of purity, banishment at 13th; hold monster, scrying at 17th.

features.name: Channel Divinity
features.description: You gain two Channel Divinity options: Watcher's Will (as an action, you can choose a number of creatures within 30 feet, up to your Charisma modifier. For 1 minute, you and they have advantage on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws) and Abjure the Extraplanar (as an action, you present your holy symbol and each aberration, celestial, elemental, fey, or fiend within 30 feet must make a Wisdom save or be turned for 1 minute).

features.name: Aura of the Sentinel
features.description: You emit an aura of alertness. When you and any creatures of your choice within 10 feet roll initiative, you all gain a bonus to initiative equal to your proficiency bonus. At 18th level, the range increases to 30 feet.

features.name: Vigilant Rebuke
features.description: Whenever you or a creature you can see within 30 feet succeeds on an Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma saving throw, you can use your reaction to deal 2d8 + your Charisma modifier force damage to the creature that forced the save.

features.name: Mortal Bulwark
features.description: As a bonus action, you gain benefits for 1 minute: truesight with a range of 120 feet, advantage on attack rolls against aberrations, celestials, elementals, fey, and fiends. When you hit one with an attack, you can force it to make a Charisma save or be banished to its native plane. Once used, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest, unless you expend a 5th-level spell slot.

features.name: Hunter's Prey
features.description: Choose one of the following: Colossus Slayer (once per turn, deal an extra 1d8 damage to a creature below its hit point maximum), Giant Killer (when a Large or larger creature within 5 feet hits or misses you, you can use your reaction to attack it), or Horde Breaker (once on each of your turns when you make a weapon attack, you can make another attack with the same weapon against a different creature within 5 feet of the original target).

features.name: Defensive Tactics
features.description: Choose one: Escape the Horde (opportunity attacks against you are made with disadvantage), Multiattack Defense (when a creature hits you with an attack, you gain +4 AC against its subsequent attacks for the rest of the turn), or Steel Will (advantage on saves against being frightened).

features.name: Multiattack
features.description: Choose one: Volley (use your action to make a ranged attack against any number of creatures within 10 feet of a point you can see, using ammunition for each) or Whirlwind Attack (use your action to make a melee attack against any number of creatures within 5 feet of you).

features.name: Superior Hunter's Defense
features.description: Choose one: Evasion (Dexterity saves for half damage: no damage on success, half on failure), Stand Against the Tide (when a hostile creature misses you with a melee attack, you can use your reaction to force it to repeat the attack against another creature), or Uncanny Dodge (when an attacker you can see hits you, you can use your reaction to halve the damage).

features.name: Ranger's Companion
features.description: You gain a beast companion that accompanies you on your adventures. Choose a beast that is no larger than Medium with CR 1/4 or lower. Add your proficiency bonus to its AC, attack rolls, damage rolls, saving throws, and skills. Its hit point maximum equals its normal maximum or four times your ranger level, whichever is higher. It obeys your commands. On your turn, you can verbally command it to move, and you can use your action to command it to take the Attack, Dash, Disengage, Dodge, or Help action. While traveling in your favored terrain with only the beast, you can move stealthily at a normal pace. If the beast dies, you can spend 8 hours magically bonding with another.

features.name: Exceptional Training
features.description: On any of your turns when your beast companion doesn't attack, you can use a bonus action to command it to take the Dash, Disengage, Dodge, or Help action.

features.name: Bestial Fury
features.description: Your beast companion can make two attacks when you command it to use the Attack action.

features.name: Share Spells
features.description: When you cast a spell targeting yourself, you can also affect your beast companion with the spell if it is within 30 feet of you.

features.name: Gloom Stalker Magic
features.description: You learn an additional spell when you reach certain levels: disguise self at 3rd, rope trick at 5th, fear at 9th, greater invisibility at 13th, seeming at 17th. These count as ranger spells.

features.name: Dread Ambusher
features.description: You gain a bonus to initiative rolls equal to your Wisdom modifier. At the start of your first turn of each combat, your walking speed increases by 10 feet, which lasts until the end of that turn. If you take the Attack action on that turn, you can make one additional weapon attack as part of that action. If that attack hits, the target takes an extra 1d8 damage.

features.name: Umbral Sight
features.description: You gain darkvision out to 60 feet. If you already have darkvision, its range increases by 30 feet. You are also adept at evading creatures that rely on darkvision. While in darkness, you are invisible to any creature that relies on darkvision to see you in that darkness.

features.name: Iron Mind
features.description: You gain proficiency in Wisdom saving throws. If you already have this proficiency, you instead gain proficiency in Intelligence or Charisma saving throws (your choice).

features.name: Stalker's Flurry
features.description: Once on each of your turns when you miss with a weapon attack, you can make another weapon attack as part of the same action.

features.name: Shadowy Dodge
features.description: Whenever a creature makes an attack roll against you and doesn't have advantage on the roll, you can use your reaction to impose disadvantage on it. You must use this feature before you know the outcome of the attack roll.

features.name: Horizon Walker Magic
features.description: You learn an additional spell when you reach certain levels: protection from evil and good at 3rd, misty step at 5th, haste at 9th, banishment at 13th, teleportation circle at 17th. These count as ranger spells.

features.name: Detect Portal
features.description: As an action, you detect the distance and direction to the closest planar portal within 1 mile of you. Once you use this, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

features.name: Planar Warrior
features.description: As a bonus action, choose one creature within 30 feet. The next time you hit that creature on this turn with a weapon attack, all damage dealt by the attack becomes force damage, and the creature takes an extra 1d8 force damage. At 11th level, the extra damage increases to 2d8.

features.name: Ethereal Step
features.description: As a bonus action, you can cast the etherealness spell with this feature, without expending a spell slot, but the spell ends at the end of the current turn. Once you use this, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

features.name: Distant Strike
features.description: When you take the Attack action, you can teleport up to 10 feet before each attack to an unoccupied space you can see. If you attack at least two different creatures with the action, you can make one additional attack with it against a third creature.

features.name: Spectral Defense
features.description: When you take damage from an attack, you can use your reaction to give yourself resistance to all of that attack's damage on this turn.

features.name: Monster Slayer Magic
features.description: You learn an additional spell when you reach certain levels: protection from evil and good at 3rd, zone of truth at 5th, magic circle at 9th, banishment at 13th, hold monster at 17th. These count as ranger spells.

features.name: Hunter's Sense
features.description: As an action, choose one creature within 60 feet. You immediately learn whether it has any damage immunities, resistances, or vulnerabilities and what they are. You can use this a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier, regaining all uses after a long rest.

features.name: Slayer's Prey
features.description: As a bonus action, you designate one creature within 60 feet as the target of this feature. The first time each turn that you hit that target with a weapon attack, it takes an extra 1d6 damage. This benefit lasts until you finish a short or long rest. It ends early if you designate a different creature.

features.name: Supernatural Defense
features.description: Whenever the target of your Slayer's Prey forces you to make a saving throw and whenever you make an ability check to escape that target's grapple, add 1d6 to your roll.

features.name: Magic-User's Nemesis
features.description: When you see a creature casting a spell or teleporting within 60 feet of you, you can use your reaction to try to magically foil it. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom save or its spell or teleport fails and is wasted. Once you use this, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

features.name: Slayer's Counter
features.description: If the target of your Slayer's Prey forces you to make a saving throw, you can use your reaction to make one weapon attack against it. You make this attack immediately before making the saving throw. If your attack hits, your save automatically succeeds, in addition to the attack's normal effects.

features.name: Dreadful Strikes
features.description: When you hit a creature with a weapon, you can deal an extra 1d4 psychic damage to the target, which can take this extra damage only once per turn. The extra damage increases to 1d6 at 11th level.

features.name: Fey Wanderer Magic
features.description: You learn an additional spell when you reach certain levels: charm person at 3rd, misty step at 5th, dispel magic at 9th, dimension door at 13th, mislead at 17th. These count as ranger spells.

features.name: Otherworldly Glamour
features.description: Whenever you make a Charisma check, you gain a bonus to the check equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of +1). In addition, you gain proficiency in one of the following skills: Deception, Performance, or Persuasion.

features.name: Beguiling Twist
features.description: You have advantage on saves against being charmed or frightened. Whenever you or a creature you can see within 120 feet succeeds on a save against being charmed or frightened, you can use your reaction to force a different creature within 120 feet to make a Wisdom save. If it fails, it is charmed or frightened by you for 1 minute.

features.name: Fey Reinforcements
features.description: You know summon fey. It doesn't count against your spells known, and you can cast it without a material component. You can also cast it once without a spell slot, regaining the ability to do so after a long rest. When you start casting it, you can modify it so that it doesn't require concentration, and its duration becomes 1 minute.

features.name: Misty Wanderer
features.description: You can cast misty step without expending a spell slot a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier, regaining all uses after a long rest. When you cast misty step, you can bring along one willing creature you can see within 5 feet. That creature teleports to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of your destination.

features.name: Gathered Swarm
features.description: A swarm of intangible nature spirits has bonded to you. Once on each of your turns, you can cause the swarm to assist you in one of the following ways, immediately after you hit a creature with an attack: the target takes 1d6 piercing damage; the target must succeed on a Strength save or be moved by the swarm up to 15 feet horizontally; you are moved by the swarm 5 feet horizontally.

features.name: Swarmkeeper Magic
features.description: You learn the mage hand cantrip if you don't already know it. You also learn an additional spell when you reach certain levels: faerie fire at 3rd, web at 5th, gaseous form at 9th, arcane eye at 13th, insect plague at 17th.

features.name: Writhing Tide
features.description: As a bonus action, you gain a flying speed of 10 feet and can hover for 1 minute. You can use this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all uses after a long rest.

features.name: Mighty Swarm
features.description: The damage of Gathered Swarm increases to 1d8. If a creature fails its save against being moved, you can also cause the swarm to knock the creature prone. When you are moved by Gathered Swarm, it gives you half cover until the start of your next turn.

features.name: Swarming Dispersal
features.description: When you take damage, you can use your reaction to give yourself resistance to that damage. You vanish into your swarm and then teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet. You can use this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all uses after a long rest.

features.name: Fast Hands
features.description: You can use the bonus action granted by your Cunning Action to make a Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check, use your thieves' tools to disarm a trap or open a lock, or take the Use an Object action.

features.name: Second-Story Work
features.description: Climbing no longer costs you extra movement. When you make a running jump, the distance you cover increases by a number of feet equal to your Dexterity modifier.

features.name: Supreme Sneak
features.description: You have advantage on a Dexterity (Stealth) check if you move no more than half your speed on the same turn.

features.name: Use Magic Device
features.description: You ignore all class, race, and level requirements on the use of magic items.

features.name: Thief's Reflexes
features.description: You can take two turns during the first round of any combat. You take your first turn at your normal initiative and your second turn at your initiative minus 10. You can't use this if surprised.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: You gain proficiency with the disguise kit and the poisoner's kit.

features.name: Assassinate
features.description: You have advantage on attack rolls against any creature that hasn't taken a turn in combat yet. Any hit you score against a surprised creature is a critical hit.

features.name: Infiltration Expertise
features.description: You can unfailingly create false identities for yourself. You must spend seven days and 25 gp to establish the history, profession, and affiliations for an identity. Thereafter, if you adopt the new identity as a disguise, other creatures believe you to be that person until given an obvious reason not to.

features.name: Impostor
features.description: You gain the ability to unerringly mimic another person's speech, writing, and behavior. You must spend at least three hours studying them. Your ruse is indiscernible to the casual observer. If a wary creature suspects, you have advantage on any Charisma (Deception) check to avoid detection.

features.name: Death Strike
features.description: When you attack and hit a creature that is surprised, it must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 + your Dexterity modifier + your proficiency bonus). On a failed save, double the damage of your attack.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You learn wizard spells, primarily from the enchantment and illusion schools. You have cantrips and spell slots as shown in the Arcane Trickster Spellcasting table. You use Intelligence as your spellcasting ability.

features.name: Mage Hand Legerdemain
features.description: When you cast mage hand, you can make the spectral hand invisible, and you can perform the following additional tasks with it: stow or retrieve an object from a container worn or carried by another creature, use thieves' tools to pick locks and disarm traps at range. You can perform one of these tasks without being noticed if you succeed on a Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check contested by the creature's Wisdom (Perception) check. Also, you can use your Cunning Action to control the hand.

features.name: Magical Ambush
features.description: If you are hidden from a creature when you cast a spell on it, the creature has disadvantage on any saving throw it makes against the spell this turn.

features.name: Versatile Trickster
features.description: As a bonus action, you can designate a creature within 5 feet of your mage hand. Doing so gives you advantage on attack rolls against that creature until the end of the turn.

features.name: Spellthief
features.description: Immediately after a creature casts a spell that targets you or includes you in its area, you can use your reaction to force it to make a saving throw with its spellcasting ability modifier. On a failure, you negate the spell's effect against you, and you steal the knowledge of the spell if it is at least 1st level and of a level you can cast. For the next 8 hours, you know the spell and can cast it using your spell slots. The creature can't cast that spell for 8 hours. Once used, you must finish a long rest before using again.

features.name: Ear for Deceit
features.description: Whenever you make a Wisdom (Insight) check to determine whether a creature is lying, treat a roll of 7 or lower on the d20 as an 8.

features.name: Eye for Detail
features.description: You can use a bonus action to make a Wisdom (Perception) check to spot a hidden creature or object or to make an Intelligence (Investigation) check to uncover or decipher clues.

features.name: Insightful Fighting
features.description: As a bonus action, you can make a Wisdom (Insight) check against a creature you can see, contested by the target's Charisma (Deception) check. If you succeed, you can use your Sneak Attack against that target even if you don't have advantage on the attack roll, but not if you have disadvantage on it. This benefit lasts for 1 minute or until you successfully use this feature against a different target.

features.name: Steady Eye
features.description: You have advantage on any Wisdom (Perception) or Intelligence (Investigation) check if you move no more than half your speed on the same turn.

features.name: Unerring Eye
features.description: As an action, you sense the presence of illusions, shapechangers not in their original form, and other magic designed to deceive the senses within 30 feet, provided you aren't blinded or deafened. You can use this a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier, regaining all uses after a long rest.

features.name: Eye for Weakness
features.description: While your Insightful Fighting feature applies to a creature, your Sneak Attack damage against that creature increases by 3d6.

features.name: Master of Intrigue
features.description: You gain proficiency with the disguise kit, the forgery kit, and one gaming set of your choice. You also learn two languages of your choice. Additionally, you can unerringly mimic the speech patterns and accent of a creature that you hear speak for at least 1 minute.

features.name: Master of Tactics
features.description: You can use the Help action as a bonus action. Additionally, when you use the Help action to aid an ally in attacking a creature, the target can be within 30 feet of you, rather than within 5 feet, if the target can see or hear you.

features.name: Insightful Manipulator
features.description: If you spend at least 1 minute observing or interacting with another creature outside combat, you can learn certain information about its capabilities compared to your own. The DM tells you if the creature is your equal, superior, or inferior in two of the following characteristics of your choice: Intelligence, Wisdom, Charisma, class levels (if any).

features.name: Misdirection
features.description: When you are targeted by an attack while a creature within 5 feet is granting you cover against that attack, you can use your reaction to have the attack target that creature instead.

features.name: Soul of Deceit
features.description: Your thoughts can't be read by telepathy or other means, unless you allow it. You can present false thoughts by succeeding on a Charisma (Deception) check contested by the mind reader's Wisdom (Insight) check. Additionally, no matter what you say, magic that would determine if you are telling the truth indicates you are being truthful if you so choose, and you can't be compelled to tell the truth by magic.

features.name: Skirmisher
features.description: You can move up to half your speed as a reaction when an enemy ends its turn within 5 feet of you. This movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks.

features.name: Survivalist
features.description: You gain proficiency in the Nature and Survival skills if you don't already have it. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of those proficiencies.

features.name: Superior Mobility
features.description: Your walking speed increases by 10 feet. If you have a climbing or swimming speed, this increase applies to that speed as well.

features.name: Ambush Master
features.description: You have advantage on initiative rolls. In addition, the first creature you hit during the first round of a combat becomes easier for you and others to strike; attack rolls against that target have advantage until the start of your next turn.

features.name: Sudden Strike
features.description: If you take the Attack action on your turn, you can make one additional attack as a bonus action. This attack can benefit from your Sneak Attack even if you have already used it this turn, but you can't use your Sneak Attack against the same target more than once in a turn.

features.name: Fancy Footwork
features.description: During your turn, if you make a melee attack against a creature, that creature can't make opportunity attacks against you for the rest of your turn.

features.name: Rakish Audacity
features.description: You can give yourself a bonus to your initiative rolls equal to your Charisma modifier. You also gain an additional way to use your Sneak Attack; you don't need advantage on the attack roll if you are within 5 feet of the target, no other creatures are within 5 feet of you, and you don't have disadvantage on the attack roll.

features.name: Panache
features.description: As an action, you can make a Charisma (Persuasion) check contested by a creature's Wisdom (Insight) check. If you succeed and the creature is hostile, it has disadvantage on attack rolls against targets other than you and can't make opportunity attacks against them. If you succeed and the creature isn't hostile, it is charmed by you for 1 minute.

features.name: Elegant Maneuver
features.description: You can use a bonus action on your turn to gain advantage on the next Dexterity (Acrobatics) or Strength (Athletics) check you make during the same turn.

features.name: Master Duellist
features.description: If you miss with an attack roll, you can roll it again with advantage. Once you do so, you can't use this feature again until you finish a short or long rest.

features.name: Whispers of the Dead
features.description: Echoes of those who have died cling to you. Whenever you finish a short or long rest, you can choose one skill or tool proficiency that you lack and gain it. You lose this proficiency when you use this feature to choose a different proficiency.

features.name: Wails from the Grave
features.description: Immediately after you deal your Sneak Attack damage to a creature on your turn, you can target a second creature within 30 feet. Roll half the number of Sneak Attack dice (round up), and the second creature takes necrotic damage equal to the total. You can use this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all uses after a long rest.

features.name: Tokens of the Departed
features.description: When a creature dies within 30 feet, you can use your reaction to cause a Tiny soul trinket to appear in your free hand. You can have a maximum number of soul trinkets equal to your proficiency bonus. While a soul trinket is on your person, you have advantage on death saves and Con saves. When you deal Sneak Attack damage, you can destroy one trinket to immediately use Wails from the Grave without expending a use. As an action, you can destroy a trinket to ask the spirit one question, which it must answer concisely (but not necessarily truthfully).

features.name: Ghost Walk
features.description: As a bonus action, you assume a spectral form for 10 minutes. While in this form, you have a flying speed of 10 feet, can hover, and attack rolls have disadvantage against you. You can also move through creatures and objects, but you take 1d10 force damage if you end your turn inside one. To use this again, you must finish a long rest or destroy a soul trinket as part of the bonus action.

features.name: Death's Friend
features.description: When you use Wails from the Grave, you can deal the necrotic damage to both the first and the second creature. At the end of a long rest, a soul trinket appears in your hand if you don't have any.

features.name: Psionic Power
features.description: You have Psionic Energy dice equal to twice your proficiency bonus (d6s). They fuel your powers. Regain all after a long rest. As a bonus action, you can regain one die after a short or long rest. Powers: Psi-Bolstered Knack (if you fail an ability check using a skill or tool you're proficient with, you can roll a die and add it to the check, expending it only if the roll succeeds) and Psychic Whispers (as an action, choose up to your proficiency bonus creatures you can see, roll a die; for hours equal to the number rolled, you can speak telepathically with them within 1 mile).

features.name: Psychic Blades
features.description: Whenever you take the Attack action, you can manifest a psychic blade from your free hand and make the attack with that blade. It is a simple melee weapon with finesse and thrown (range 60). On a hit, it deals psychic damage equal to 1d6 + the ability modifier you used for the attack roll. After you attack with the blade, you can make a second attack with another psychic blade as a bonus action, dealing 1d4 damage.

features.name: Soul Blades
features.description: Your Psychic Blades gain new powers: Homing Strikes (if you miss with a Psychic Blade attack, you can roll a Psionic Energy die and add it to the attack roll, expending the die if it hits) and Psychic Teleportation (as a bonus action, you manifest a blade, expend a die, and teleport to an unoccupied space within 10 times the number rolled).

features.name: Psychic Veil
features.description: As an action, you can become invisible for 1 hour, until you dismiss it, or until you deal damage or force a save. Once used, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest, unless you expend a Psionic Energy die to use it again.

features.name: Rend Mind
features.description: When you use your Psychic Blades to deal Sneak Attack damage, you can force the target to make a Wisdom save. If it fails, it is stunned for 1 minute. It can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns. Once used, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest, unless you expend three Psionic Energy dice to use it again.

features.name: Dragon Ancestor
features.description: You choose one type of dragon as your ancestor. The damage type associated with each dragon is used by features you gain later. You can speak, read, and write Draconic. Additionally, whenever you make a Charisma check when interacting with dragons, your proficiency bonus is doubled.

features.name: Draconic Resilience
features.description: Your hit point maximum increases by 1 and increases by 1 again whenever you gain a level. Also, parts of your skin are covered by a thin sheen of dragon-like scales. When you aren't wearing armor, your AC equals 13 + your Dexterity modifier.

features.name: Elemental Affinity
features.description: When you cast a spell that deals damage of the type associated with your draconic ancestry, add your Charisma modifier to that damage. At the same time, you can spend 1 sorcery point to gain resistance to that damage type for 1 hour.

features.name: Dragon Wings
features.description: You gain the ability to sprout a pair of dragon wings from your back, gaining a flying speed equal to your current speed. You can create these wings as a bonus action on your turn, and they last until you dismiss them as a bonus action.

features.name: Draconic Presence
features.description: As an action, you can spend 5 sorcery points to exude an aura of awe or fear (your choice) to a distance of 60 feet. For 1 minute or until you lose concentration, each hostile creature that starts its turn in the aura must succeed on a Wisdom save or be charmed (if awe) or frightened (if fear) until the aura ends. A creature that succeeds is immune for 24 hours.

features.name: Wild Magic Surge
features.description: Immediately after you cast a sorcerer spell of 1st level or higher, the DM can have you roll a d20. If you roll a 1, roll on the Wild Magic Surge table to create a random magical effect.

features.name: Tides of Chaos
features.description: You can manipulate the forces of chance and chaos to gain advantage on one attack roll, ability check, or saving throw. Once you do so, you must finish a long rest before using it again. Any time before you regain the use of this feature, the DM can have you roll on the Wild Magic Surge table immediately after you cast a sorcerer spell of 1st level or higher. You then regain the use of this feature.

features.name: Bend Luck
features.description: When another creature you can see makes an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, you can use your reaction and spend 2 sorcery points to roll 1d4 and apply the number as a bonus or penalty to the creature's roll. You can do so after the creature rolls but before any effects of the roll occur.

features.name: Controlled Chaos
features.description: Whenever you roll on the Wild Magic Surge table, you can roll twice and use either number.

features.name: Spell Bombardment
features.description: When you roll damage for a spell and roll the highest number possible on any of the dice, choose one of those dice, roll it again and add that roll to the damage. You can use this feature only once per turn.

features.name: Divine Magic
features.description: Your link to the divine allows you to learn spells from the cleric class. When you learn or replace a sorcerer cantrip or spell, you can choose it from the cleric spell list or the sorcerer spell list. Choose an affinity for your divine power: good (cure wounds), evil (inflict wounds), law (bless), chaos (bane), or neutrality (protection from evil and good).

features.name: Favored by the Gods
features.description: If you fail a saving throw or miss with an attack roll, you can roll 2d4 and add it to the total, possibly changing the outcome. Once you use this, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

features.name: Empowered Healing
features.description: Whenever you or an ally within 5 feet rolls dice to determine the number of hit points a spell restores, you can spend 1 sorcery point to reroll any number of those dice once, provided you aren't incapacitated. You can use this only once per turn.

features.name: Otherworldly Wings
features.description: You can use a bonus action to manifest a pair of spectral wings from your back. While the wings are present, you have a flying speed of 30 feet. The wings last until you're incapacitated, you die, or you dismiss them as a bonus action. The appearance depends on your affinity.

features.name: Unearthly Recovery
features.description: As a bonus action when you have fewer than half of your hit points remaining, you can regain a number of hit points equal to half your hit point maximum. Once you use this, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Eyes of the Dark
features.description: You have darkvision with a range of 120 feet. At 3rd level, you learn the darkness spell, which doesn't count against your spells known. You can cast it by spending 2 sorcery points or by expending a spell slot. If you cast it with sorcery points, you can see through the darkness created by the spell.

features.name: Strength of the Grave
features.description: When damage reduces you to 0 hit points, you can make a Charisma saving throw (DC 5 + the damage taken). On a success, you instead drop to 1 hit point. You can't use this if reduced to 0 by radiant damage or a critical hit. After it succeeds, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Hound of Ill Omen
features.description: As a bonus action, you can spend 3 sorcery points to summon a hound of ill omen to target one creature within 120 feet. The hound uses the dire wolf's statistics, but is size Medium, counts as a monstrosity, and has temporary HP equal to half your sorcerer level. It can move through other creatures and objects. At the start of its turn, it knows its target's location. On its turn, it can only move toward and attack its target. While the hound is within 5 feet of the target, the target has disadvantage on saves against your spells.

features.name: Shadow Walk
features.description: When you are in dim light or darkness, as a bonus action, you can teleport up to 120 feet to an unoccupied space you can see that is also in dim light or darkness.

features.name: Umbral Form
features.description: As a bonus action, you can spend 6 sorcery points to transform into a shadowy form for 1 minute. In this form, you have resistance to all damage except force and radiant, and you can move through other creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain. You take 5 force damage if you end your turn inside an object.

features.name: Wind Speaker
features.description: You can speak, read, and write Primordial.

features.name: Tempestuous Magic
features.description: You can use a bonus action on your turn to cause whirling gusts of elemental air to briefly surround you, immediately before or after you cast a spell of 1st level or higher. Doing so allows you to fly up to 10 feet without provoking opportunity attacks.

features.name: Heart of the Storm
features.description: You gain resistance to lightning and thunder damage. In addition, whenever you start casting a spell of 1st level or higher that deals lightning or thunder damage, stormy magic erupts from you. This eruption causes creatures of your choice that you can see within 10 feet to take lightning or thunder damage (choose each time) equal to half your sorcerer level.

features.name: Storm Guide
features.description: If it is raining, you can use an action to cause the rain to stop falling in a 20-foot radius sphere centered on you. If it is windy, you can use a bonus action each round to choose the direction that the wind blows in a 100-foot radius sphere centered on you.

features.name: Storm's Fury
features.description: When you are hit by a melee attack, you can use your reaction to deal lightning damage to the attacker equal to your sorcerer level. The attacker must also make a Strength save or be pushed up to 20 feet away.

features.name: Wind Soul
features.description: You gain immunity to lightning and thunder damage. You also gain a magical flying speed of 60 feet. As an action, you can reduce your flying speed to 30 feet for 1 hour and choose a number of creatures within 30 feet equal to 3 + your Charisma modifier. They gain a magical flying speed of 30 feet for 1 hour. Once you reduce your flying speed in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

features.name: Psionic Spells
features.description: You learn additional spells when you reach certain levels: arms of Hadar, dissonant whispers, mind sliver at 1st; calm emotions, detect thoughts at 3rd; hunger of Hadar, sending at 5th; Evard's black tentacles, summon aberration at 7th; Rary's telepathic bond, telekinesis at 9th. They count as sorcerer spells but not against your spells known. You can replace one with a divination or enchantment spell from any class.

features.name: Telepathic Speech
features.description: As a bonus action, choose one creature you can see within 30 feet. You and it can speak telepathically while within a number of miles equal to your Charisma modifier. It lasts a number of minutes equal to your sorcerer level.

features.name: Psionic Sorcery
features.description: When you cast any spell of 1st level or higher from your Psionic Spells feature, you can cast it by expending a spell slot as normal or by spending sorcery points equal to the spell's level. If you cast it with sorcery points, it requires no verbal, somatic, or material components (unless they are consumed).

features.name: Psychic Defenses
features.description: You gain resistance to psychic damage, and you have advantage on saving throws against being charmed or frightened.

features.name: Revelation in Flesh
features.description: As a bonus action, you can spend 1 or more sorcery points to magically transform your body for 10 minutes. For each point, you gain one benefit: see any invisible creature within 60 feet; flying speed equal to walking speed; swimming speed equal to twice walking speed and underwater breathing; move through any space as narrow as 1 inch without squeezing.

features.name: Warping Implosion
features.description: As an action, you can teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 120 feet. Immediately after you disappear, each creature within 30 feet of the space you left must make a Strength save. On a failure, they take 3d10 force damage and are pulled toward the space. Once used, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest, unless you spend 5 sorcery points to use it again.

features.name: Clockwork Magic
features.description: You learn additional spells when you reach certain levels: alarm, protection from evil and good at 1st; aid, lesser restoration at 3rd; dispel magic, protection from energy at 5th; freedom of movement, summon construct at 7th; greater restoration, wall of force at 9th. They count as sorcerer spells but not against your spells known. You can replace one with an abjuration or transmutation spell from any class.

features.name: Restore Balance
features.description: When a creature you can see within 60 feet is about to roll a d20 with advantage or disadvantage, you can use your reaction to prevent the roll from being affected by advantage and disadvantage. You can use this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all uses after a long rest.

features.name: Bastion of Law
features.description: As an action, you can expend 1 to 5 sorcery points to create a magical ward around yourself or another creature within 30 feet. The ward is represented by a number of d8s equal to the number of sorcery points spent. When the warded creature takes damage, it can expend a number of those dice, roll them, and reduce the damage taken by the total rolled.

features.name: Trance of Order
features.description: As a bonus action, you can enter this state for 1 minute. For the duration, attack rolls against you can't benefit from advantage, and whenever you make an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, you can treat a roll of 9 or lower on the d20 as a 10. Once used, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest, unless you spend 5 sorcery points to use it again.

features.name: Clockwork Cavalcade
features.description: As an action, you summon spirits of order in a 30-foot cube originating from you. The spirits are intangible and invulnerable. They restore up to 100 hit points, divided as you choose among any number of creatures in the cube. Any damaged objects in the cube are repaired instantly. Every spell of 6th level or lower ends on creatures and objects of your choice in the cube. Once used, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest, unless you spend 7 sorcery points to use it again.

features.name: Expanded Spell List
features.description: You gain additional spells: faerie fire, sleep at 1st; calm emotions, phantasmal force at 3rd; blink, plant growth at 5th; dominate beast, greater invisibility at 7th; dominate person, seeming at 9th.

features.name: Fey Presence
features.description: As an action, you can cause each creature in a 10-foot cube originating from you to make a Wisdom save. Those that fail are charmed or frightened by you (your choice) until the end of your next turn. Once used, you must finish a short or long rest before using again.

features.name: Misty Escape
features.description: When you take damage, you can use your reaction to turn invisible and teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. You remain invisible until the start of your next turn or until you attack or cast a spell. Once used, you must finish a short or long rest before using again.

features.name: Beguiling Defenses
features.description: You are immune to being charmed. When another creature attempts to charm you, you can use your reaction to turn the charm back on it. It must succeed on a Wisdom save or be charmed by you for 1 minute or until it takes damage.

features.name: Dark Delirium
features.description: As an action, choose a creature you can see within 60 feet. It must make a Wisdom save or be charmed or frightened by you for 1 minute or until your concentration is broken. Until this illusion ends, the creature thinks it is lost in a misty realm, seeing and hearing only itself, you, and the illusion. Once used, you must finish a short or long rest before using again.

features.name: Expanded Spell List
features.description: You gain additional spells: burning hands, command at 1st; blindness/deafness, scorching ray at 3rd; fireball, stinking cloud at 5th; fire shield, wall of fire at 7th; flame strike, hallow at 9th.

features.name: Dark One's Blessing
features.description: When you reduce a hostile creature to 0 hit points, you gain temporary hit points equal to your Charisma modifier + your warlock level (minimum 1).

features.name: Dark One's Own Luck
features.description: When you make an ability check or a saving throw, you can use this feature to add a d10 to your roll. You can do so after seeing the initial roll but before any effects. Once used, you must finish a short or long rest before using again.

features.name: Fiendish Resilience
features.description: You can choose one damage type when you finish a short or long rest. You gain resistance to that damage type until you choose a different one. Damage from magical weapons or silver weapons ignores this resistance.

features.name: Hurl Through Hell
features.description: When you hit a creature with an attack, you can use this feature to transport it through the lower planes. At the end of your next turn, it returns. If it is not a fiend, it takes 10d10 psychic damage. Once used, you must finish a long rest before using again.

features.name: Expanded Spell List
features.description: You gain additional spells: dissonant whispers, Tasha's hideous laughter at 1st; detect thoughts, phantasmal force at 3rd; clairvoyance, sending at 5th; dominate beast, Evard's black tentacles at 7th; dominate person, telekinesis at 9th.

features.name: Awakened Mind
features.description: You can communicate telepathically with any creature you can see within 30 feet. You don't need to share a language, but it must be able to understand at least one language.

features.name: Entropic Ward
features.description: When a creature makes an attack roll against you, you can use your reaction to impose disadvantage. If the attack misses, your next attack roll against that creature has advantage if made before the end of your next turn. Once used, you must finish a short or long rest before using again.

features.name: Thought Shield
features.description: Your thoughts can't be read by telepathy or other means unless you allow it. You also have resistance to psychic damage, and whenever a creature deals psychic damage to you, that creature takes the same amount.

features.name: Create Thrall
features.description: You can use your action to touch an incapacitated humanoid. That creature is then charmed by you until a remove curse spell is cast on it, the charmed condition is removed, or you use this feature again. You can communicate telepathically with the charmed creature as long as you are on the same plane.

features.name: Expanded Spell List
features.description: You gain additional spells: cure wounds, guiding bolt at 1st; flaming sphere, lesser restoration at 3rd; daylight, revivify at 5th; guardian of faith, wall of fire at 7th; flame strike, greater restoration at 9th.

features.name: Bonus Cantrips
features.description: You learn the light and sacred flame cantrips. They count as warlock cantrips for you, but they don't count against your number of cantrips known.

features.name: Healing Light
features.description: You have a pool of d6s equal to 1 + your warlock level. As a bonus action, you can heal one creature you can see within 60 feet, spending dice from the pool. The maximum number of dice you can spend at once equals your Charisma modifier. Your pool regains all dice after a long rest.

features.name: Radiant Soul
features.description: You have resistance to radiant damage. When you cast a spell that deals radiant or fire damage, you can add your Charisma modifier to one radiant or fire damage roll of that spell against one of its targets.

features.name: Celestial Resilience
features.description: You gain temporary hit points whenever you finish a short or long rest equal to your warlock level + your Charisma modifier. Additionally, choose up to five creatures you can see at the end of the rest. They each gain temporary hit points equal to half your warlock level + your Charisma modifier.

features.name: Searing Vengeance
features.description: When you have to make a death saving throw at the start of your turn, you can instead spring back to your feet with a burst of radiant energy. You regain hit points equal to half your hit point maximum, stand up, and each creature of your choice within 30 feet takes radiant damage equal to 2d8 + your Charisma modifier and is blinded until the end of the current turn. Once used, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Expanded Spell List
features.description: You gain additional spells: shield, wrathful smite at 1st; blur, branding smite at 3rd; blink, elemental weapon at 5th; phantasmal killer, staggering smite at 7th; banishing smite, cone of cold at 9th.

features.name: Hexblade's Curse
features.description: As a bonus action, choose one creature you can see within 30 feet. The target is cursed for 1 minute. Until the curse ends, you gain a bonus to damage rolls against it equal to your proficiency bonus, any attack roll against it is a critical hit on a 19 or 20, and if it dies, you regain hit points equal to your warlock level + your Charisma modifier. Once used, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

features.name: Hex Warrior
features.description: You gain proficiency with medium armor, shields, and martial weapons. Whenever you finish a long rest, you can touch one weapon that you are proficient with and that lacks the two-handed property. When you attack with that weapon, you can use your Charisma modifier, instead of Strength or Dexterity, for the attack and damage rolls. This benefit lasts until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Accursed Specter
features.description: When you slay a humanoid, you can cause its spirit to rise from its corpse as a specter. It gains temporary hit points equal to half your warlock level, obeys your verbal commands, and gets a bonus to its attack rolls equal to your Charisma modifier. It remains until the end of your next long rest. Once you bind a specter, you can't use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Armor of Hexes
features.description: If the target cursed by your Hexblade's Curse hits you with an attack roll, you can use your reaction to roll a d6. On a 4 or higher, the attack instead misses you, regardless of its roll.

features.name: Master of Hexes
features.description: When the creature cursed by your Hexblade's Curse dies, you can apply the curse to a different creature you can see within 30 feet, provided you aren't incapacitated. When you apply the curse in this way, you don't regain hit points from the death of the previously cursed creature.

features.name: Expanded Spell List
features.description: You gain additional spells: create or destroy water, thunderwave at 1st; gust of wind, silence at 3rd; lightning bolt, sleet storm at 5th; control water, summon elemental (water only) at 7th; Bigby's hand (appears as a tentacle), cone of cold at 9th.

features.name: Tentacle of the Deeps
features.description: As a bonus action, you create a 10-foot-long tentacle at a point within 60 feet. The tentacle lasts for 1 minute. When you create it, you can make a melee spell attack against one creature within 10 feet of it. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 cold damage, and its speed is reduced by 10 feet until the start of your next turn. As a bonus action, you can move it up to 30 feet and repeat the attack. You can summon the tentacle a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus.

features.name: Gift of the Sea
features.description: You gain a swimming speed of 40 feet, and you can breathe underwater.

features.name: Oceanic Soul
features.description: You gain resistance to cold damage. In addition, when you are fully submerged, any creature that is also fully submerged can understand your speech, and you can understand theirs.

features.name: Guardian Coil
features.description: When you or a creature you can see takes damage while within 10 feet of your Tentacle of the Deeps, you can use your reaction to reduce the damage to that creature by 1d8.

features.name: Grasping Tentacles
features.description: You learn Evard's black tentacles. It counts as a warlock spell for you, and you can cast it once without a spell slot, regaining the ability after a long rest. Whenever you cast it, you gain temporary hit points equal to your warlock level, and damage can't break your concentration on it.

features.name: Fathomless Plunge
features.description: As an action, you can teleport yourself and up to five willing creatures within 30 feet up to 1 mile away to a body of water you've seen. Once used, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

features.name: Expanded Spell List
features.description: You gain additional spells based on your patron's kind: Dao (sanctuary, spike growth, meld into stone, stone shape, wall of stone), Djinni (thunderwave, gust of wind, wind wall, greater invisibility, seeming), Efreeti (burning hands, scorching ray, fireball, fire shield, flame strike), or Marid (fog cloud, blur, sleet storm, control water, cone of cold).

features.name: Genie's Vessel
features.description: Your patron gifts you a magical vessel. While touching it, you can use it as a spellcasting focus. As an action, you can vanish and enter your vessel, which remains in the space. The interior is an extradimensional space, a 20-foot-radius cylinder. You can remain inside up to hours equal to twice your proficiency bonus. You exit early as a bonus action. You can't enter again until after a long rest. Also, once during each of your turns when you hit with an attack roll, you can deal extra damage equal to your proficiency bonus. The damage type is bludgeoning (dao), thunder (djinni), fire (efreeti), or cold (marid).

features.name: Elemental Gift
features.description: You gain resistance to a damage type based on your patron's kind: bludgeoning (dao), thunder (djinni), fire (efreeti), or cold (marid). As a bonus action, you can give yourself a flying speed of 30 feet that lasts for 10 minutes. You can use this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus.

features.name: Sanctuary Vessel
features.description: When you enter your Genie's Vessel, you can now choose up to five willing creatures within 30 feet to be drawn into the vessel with you. As a bonus action, you can eject any number of creatures. Anyone who remains within the vessel for at least 10 minutes gains the benefit of finishing a short rest, and anyone can add your proficiency bonus to the number of hit points they regain if they spend any Hit Dice as part of a short rest there.

features.name: Limited Wish
features.description: As an action, you can speak your desire to your Genie's Vessel, requesting the effect of one spell that is 6th level or lower and has a casting time of 1 action. The spell can be from any class's spell list, and you don't need to meet the requirements in that spell, including costly components; the spell simply takes effect. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish 1d4 long rests.

features.name: Abjuration Savant
features.description: The gold and time you must spend to copy an abjuration spell into your spellbook is halved.

features.name: Arcane Ward
features.description: When you cast an abjuration spell of 1st level or higher, you can create a magical ward on yourself that lasts until you finish a long rest. The ward has hit points equal to twice your wizard level + your Intelligence modifier. Whenever you take damage, the ward takes the damage instead. When you cast an abjuration spell of 1st level or higher, the ward regains a number of hit points equal to twice the level of the spell.

features.name: Projected Ward
features.description: When a creature you can see within 30 feet takes damage, you can use your reaction to cause your Arcane Ward to absorb that damage.

features.name: Improved Abjuration
features.description: When you cast an abjuration spell that requires you to make an ability check (as in counterspell and dispel magic), you add your proficiency bonus to that ability check.

features.name: Spell Resistance
features.description: You have advantage on saving throws against spells. Furthermore, you have resistance against the damage of spells.

features.name: Conjuration Savant
features.description: The gold and time you must spend to copy a conjuration spell into your spellbook is halved.

features.name: Minor Conjuration
features.description: As an action, you can conjure up an inanimate object in your hand or on the ground within 10 feet. The object can be no larger than 3 feet on a side and weigh no more than 10 pounds, and its form must be that of a nonmagical object you have seen. The object is visibly magical, radiating dim light out to 5 feet. It disappears after 1 hour, when you use this feature again, or if it takes any damage.

features.name: Benign Transposition
features.description: As an action, you can teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. Alternatively, you can choose a space occupied by a Small or Medium creature. If that creature is willing, you both teleport, swapping places. Once used, you must finish a long rest or cast a conjuration spell of 1st level or higher before using again.

features.name: Focused Conjuration
features.description: While you are concentrating on a conjuration spell, your concentration can't be broken as a result of taking damage.

features.name: Durable Summons
features.description: Any creature that you summon or create with a conjuration spell has 30 temporary hit points.

features.name: Divination Savant
features.description: The gold and time you must spend to copy a divination spell into your spellbook is halved.

features.name: Portent
features.description: When you finish a long rest, roll two d20s and record the numbers. You can replace any attack roll, saving throw, or ability check made by you or a creature you can see with one of these foretelling rolls. You must choose to do so before the roll. Each foretelling roll can be used only once.

features.name: Expert Divination
features.description: When you cast a divination spell of 2nd level or higher using a spell slot, you regain one expended spell slot. The slot you regain must be of a level lower than the spell you cast and can't be higher than 5th.

features.name: The Third Eye
features.description: As an action, you can increase your powers of perception for 1 minute, choosing one of the following benefits: darkvision (60 feet), ethereal sight (see into Ethereal Plane within 60 feet), greater comprehension (read any language), or see invisibility (see invisible creatures and objects within 10 feet). You can't use this feature again until after a rest.

features.name: Greater Portent
features.description: You roll three d20s for your Portent feature, rather than two.

features.name: Enchantment Savant
features.description: The gold and time you must spend to copy an enchantment spell into your spellbook is halved.

features.name: Hypnotic Gaze
features.description: As an action, choose one creature you can see within 5 feet. If it can see or hear you, it must make a Wisdom save or be charmed until the end of your next turn. The charmed creature's speed drops to 0, and it is incapacitated and visibly dazed. On subsequent turns, you can use your action to maintain this effect, extending its duration. The effect ends if you move more than 5 feet away, if it can't see or hear you, or if it takes damage. Once a creature succeeds on its save, you can't use this on it again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Instinctive Charm
features.description: When a creature you can see within 30 feet makes an attack roll against you, you can use your reaction to divert it, provided another creature is within range. The attacker must make a Wisdom save. On failure, it must target the closest creature other than you or itself. On success, you can't use this feature on it again until after a long rest.

features.name: Split Enchantment
features.description: When you cast an enchantment spell of 1st level or higher that targets only one creature, you can have it target a second creature.

features.name: Alter Memories
features.description: When you cast an enchantment spell to charm one or more creatures, you can alter one creature's understanding so that it remains unaware of being charmed. Additionally, once before the spell expires, you can use your action to make the creature forget some of the time it spent charmed. It must make an Intelligence save or lose a number of hours of its memories equal to 1 + your Charisma modifier (minimum 1).

features.name: Evocation Savant
features.description: The gold and time you must spend to copy an evocation spell into your spellbook is halved.

features.name: Sculpt Spells
features.description: When you cast an evocation spell that affects other creatures you can see, you can choose a number of them equal to 1 + the spell's level. They automatically succeed on their saves and take no damage if they would normally take half on a success.

features.name: Potent Cantrip
features.description: When a creature succeeds on a saving throw against your cantrip, it takes half the cantrip's damage but suffers no additional effect.

features.name: Empowered Evocation
features.description: You can add your Intelligence modifier to the damage roll of any wizard evocation spell you cast.

features.name: Overchannel
features.description: You can increase the power of your simpler spells. When you cast a wizard spell of 5th level or lower that deals damage, you can deal maximum damage with that spell. The first time you do so, you suffer no adverse effect. If you use this feature again before finishing a long rest, you take 2d12 necrotic damage for each level of the spell, immediately after you cast it. Each subsequent use increases the necrotic damage per level by 1d12.

features.name: Illusion Savant
features.description: The gold and time you must spend to copy an illusion spell into your spellbook is halved.

features.name: Improved Minor Illusion
features.description: You learn the minor illusion cantrip. If you already know it, you learn a different wizard cantrip of your choice. When you cast minor illusion, you can create both a sound and an image with a single casting.

features.name: Malleable Illusions
features.description: When you cast an illusion spell that has a duration of 1 minute or longer, you can use your action to change the nature of that illusion (using the spell's normal parameters), provided that you can see the illusion.

features.name: Illusory Self
features.description: When a creature makes an attack roll against you, you can use your reaction to interpose an illusory duplicate. The attack automatically misses you, then the illusion dissipates. Once used, you must finish a short or long rest before using again.

features.name: Illusory Reality
features.description: When you cast an illusion spell of 1st level or higher, you can choose one inanimate, nonmagical object that is part of the illusion and make that object real for 1 minute. You can do this on your turn as a bonus action while the spell is ongoing. The object can't deal damage or otherwise directly harm anyone.

features.name: Necromancy Savant
features.description: The gold and time you must spend to copy a necromancy spell into your spellbook is halved.

features.name: Grim Harvest
features.description: Once per turn when you kill one or more creatures with a spell of 1st level or higher, you regain hit points equal to twice the spell's level, or three times its level if the spell is from the School of Necromancy. You don't gain this benefit for killing constructs or undead.

features.name: Undead Thralls
features.description: You add the animate dead spell to your spellbook. When you cast animate dead, you can target one additional corpse or pile of bones, creating another zombie or skeleton. Whenever you create an undead using a necromancy spell, it has additional benefits: its hit point maximum is increased by an amount equal to your wizard level, and it adds your proficiency bonus to its weapon damage rolls.

features.name: Inured to Undeath
features.description: You have resistance to necrotic damage, and your hit point maximum can't be reduced.

features.name: Command Undead
features.description: As an action, you can choose one undead you can see within 60 feet. It must make a Charisma save. On failure, it becomes friendly to you and obeys your commands until you use this feature again. Intelligent undead have advantage on the save and can repeat it at the end of every hour.

features.name: Transmutation Savant
features.description: The gold and time you must spend to copy a transmutation spell into your spellbook is halved.

features.name: Minor Alchemy
features.description: You can temporarily alter the physical properties of one nonmagical object, changing it from one substance into another. You perform a special alchemical procedure on one object composed entirely of wood, stone, iron, copper, or silver, transforming it into a different one of those materials. For each 10 minutes you spend, you can transform up to 1 cubic foot of material. After 1 hour, or until you lose concentration, the material reverts.

features.name: Transmuter's Stone
features.description: You can spend 8 hours creating a transmuter's stone that stores transmutation magic. You can benefit from the stone yourself or give it to another creature. Choose one benefit: darkvision (60 feet), increase speed by 10 feet, proficiency in Constitution saving throws, or resistance to one damage type (acid, cold, fire, lightning, or thunder). Each time you cast a transmutation spell of 1st level or higher, you can change the effect of your stone if it is on your person.

features.name: Shapechanger
features.description: You add the polymorph spell to your spellbook. You can cast polymorph without expending a spell slot, targeting only yourself and transforming into a beast with CR 1 or lower. Once used, you must finish a short or long rest before using again.

features.name: Master Transmuter
features.description: As an action, you can consume your transmuter's stone in a single burst to produce one of the following effects: Major Transformation (transmute one nonmagical object no larger than a 5-foot cube into another nonmagical object of similar size and mass, of equal or lesser value, after 10 minutes), Panacea (remove all curses, diseases, and poisons affecting a creature you touch, and it regains all hit points), Restore Life (cast raise dead without a spell slot), or Restore Youth (touch a willing creature to reduce its apparent age by 3d10 years, to a minimum of 13 years). Your stone is destroyed and can't be remade until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Arcane Deflection
features.description: When you are hit by an attack or you fail a saving throw, you can use your reaction to gain a +2 bonus to your AC against that attack or a +4 bonus to that saving throw. When you use this feature, you can't cast spells other than cantrips until the end of your next turn.

features.name: Tactical Wit
features.description: You can give yourself a bonus to your initiative rolls equal to your Intelligence modifier.

features.name: Power Surge
features.description: You can store magical energy within yourself to later empower your damaging spells. You have a number of power surges equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of one), resetting to one after a long rest. Whenever you successfully end a spell with dispel magic or counterspell, you gain one power surge. Once per turn when you deal damage with a wizard spell, you can spend one power surge to deal extra force damage equal to half your wizard level.

features.name: Durable Magic
features.description: While you maintain concentration on a spell, you have a +2 bonus to AC and all saving throws.

features.name: Deflecting Shroud
features.description: When you use your Arcane Deflection feature, you can cause magical energy to arc from you. Up to three creatures of your choice that you can see within 60 feet each take force damage equal to half your wizard level.

features.name: Training in War and Song
features.description: You gain proficiency with light armor, and you gain proficiency with one type of one-handed melee weapon of your choice. You also gain proficiency in the Performance skill.

features.name: Bladesong
features.description: You can use a bonus action to start the Bladesong, which lasts for 1 minute. It ends early if you are incapacitated, don medium or heavy armor or a shield, or use two hands to make an attack with a weapon. While active, you gain: a bonus to AC equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum +1), your walking speed increases by 10 feet, advantage on Dexterity (Acrobatics) checks, and a bonus to Constitution saves to maintain concentration equal to your Intelligence modifier.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn. Moreover, you can cast one of your cantrips in place of one of those attacks.

features.name: Song of Defense
features.description: When you take damage while your Bladesong is active, you can use your reaction to expend one spell slot and reduce that damage by an amount equal to five times the spell slot's level.

features.name: Song of Victory
features.description: You can add your Intelligence modifier (minimum of +1) to the damage of your melee weapon attacks while your Bladesong is active.

features.name: Wizardly Quill
features.description: As a bonus action, you can magically create a Tiny quill in your free hand. It doesn't require ink, and the time to copy a spell into your spellbook is 2 minutes per spell level if you use it. You can erase anything you write with it by waving the feather over the text.

features.name: Awakened Spellbook
features.description: You have awakened an arcane sentience within your spellbook. While holding it, you gain: you can use it as a spellcasting focus; when you cast a wizard spell with a spell slot, you can temporarily replace its damage type with a type that appears in another spell in your spellbook of the same level; when you cast a wizard spell as a ritual, you can use the spell's normal casting time once per long rest. You can replace the book over a short rest.

features.name: Manifest Mind
features.description: As a bonus action, you can cause the mind of your Awakened Spellbook to manifest as a Tiny spectral object within 60 feet. It is intangible, sheds dim light in a 10-foot radius, and has darkvision 60 feet. It can telepathically share what it sees and hears with you. Whenever you cast a wizard spell on your turn, you can cast it as if you were in the spectral mind's space, using its senses. You can do this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You can move it up to 30 feet as a bonus action.

features.name: Master Scrivener
features.description: Whenever you finish a long rest, you can create one magic scroll by touching your Wizardly Quill to a blank piece of paper and causing one spell of 1st or 2nd level from your spellbook to be copied onto it. The spell on the scroll counts as one level higher. You can cast it from the scroll by reading it as an action. It vanishes when you cast it or after your next long rest. The gold and time to craft spell scrolls are halved if you use your Wizardly Quill.

features.name: One with the Word
features.description: Your connection to your Awakened Spellbook has become profound. While the book is on your person, you have advantage on Intelligence (Arcana) checks. If you take damage while your spellbook's mind is manifested, you can use your reaction to dismiss the mind and prevent all of that damage to you. Then roll 3d6. The spellbook temporarily loses spells of your choice that have a combined spell level equal to that roll or higher. You can't cast the lost spells for 1d6 long rests, after which they reappear. Once you use this reaction, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.
